Pattern_raw_name: /growthkit.* slash commands (constitution, specify, clarify, plan, tasks, implement)
How_implemented: Commands are markdown files (Spec-Kit format) with YAML frontmatter declaring `description`, `scripts:` (sh + ps one-liners run via {SCRIPT}), and `handoffs:` (labeled next-agent suggestions, e.g. specify→speckit.plan/speckit.clarify with prefilled prompt + send:true). The body is a numbered execution flow the agent follows. They chain into a fixed pipeline (specify→clarify→plan→tasks→implement) — these are user-invoked /commands, not auto-triggered skills. Methodology is encoded: viral-loop taxonomy (Remix/Integration/Watermark), growth types (Vibe/Trust/Agentic), GEO answer-density.

specify.md instructs the agent to insert at most 3 [NEEDS CLARIFICATION: question] markers (prioritized scope>security>UX>technical), then surface each as a numbered question with a markdown options table (A/B/C/Custom + Implications column), present all together, wait for the user's choices, and replace markers with answers before re-validating. A dedicated /growthkit.clarify command resolves persona-sharpness / viral-loop / metric ambiguities before planning.

After writing spec.md, specify generates FEATURE_DIR/checklists/requirements.md (Content Quality / Requirement Completeness / Feature Readiness items), then runs a pass/fail review of the spec against each item, updates the spec, and re-runs up to 3 iterations until green — a self-enforced output contract. implement.md reads these checklist files, tallies [ ]/[X] into a status table, and HALTS asking yes/no if any checklist is incomplete before executing.

Each phase pairs a command file (method) with a template (structure: spec-template.md, plan-template.md, tasks-template.md) the agent must fill preserving section order; the README supplies worked examples across three fictional growth types (Vibe/Trust/Agentic — code-assistant, contract-AI, video-gen) each showing expected personas, viral loop, success metric, and k-factor target.

Pattern_raw_name: growthkit init CLI installer + automated template release
How_implemented: A typer-based Python CLI (src/growthkit_cli, installed via `uv tool install growthkit-cli` or `uvx`) scaffolds a project, prompting for agent (17 supported) and script type (sh/ps). build-templates.sh + validate-templates.sh + a GitHub Actions release.yml generate 34 template variants (17 agents × 2 scripts) from the .growthkit/ source on a version tag, validate them (no Spec-Kit leftover refs, correct structure, v1.1.0 constitution), and publish to GitHub Releases with SHA-256 checksums — cross-harness packaging baked into CI.

Single user-invocable entry skill (`/seo $1 $2`, command + url). YAML frontmatter: name, description (long trigger-keyword list for auto-invocation), user-invocable:true, argument-hint, license, metadata{author,version,category}. Body is a 28-row routing table dispatching each subcommand to one of 24 sub-skills; on `/seo audit` it delegates in parallel to 18 sub-agents. Acts as both router and the single multi-domain operator surface.

Each of the 25 sub-skills is a folder skills/<name>/ with SKILL.md (frontmatter + method), a references/ subfolder of domain knowledge files (e.g. seo-technical/references/agent-friendly-pages.md), and a bundled LICENSE.txt; execution is delegated to shared ~50 Python scripts at plugin-root scripts/. Method (SKILL.md) + reference (references/*.md) + executable (scripts/*.py) form a triad.

Pattern_raw_name: Domain decomposition into 21 core + framework + extension-mirror skills
How_implemented: SEO is sliced into 25 narrow sub-skills (seo-technical, seo-content, seo-schema, seo-sitemap, seo-geo, seo-local, seo-maps, seo-cluster, seo-sxo, seo-drift, seo-ecommerce, seo-hreflang, seo-backlinks, seo-programmatic, seo-images, seo-image-gen, seo-content-brief, seo-plan, seo-competitor-pages, seo-google, seo-dataforseo, seo-flow, ...). The orchestrating seo skill calls these workflow/component skills; extension skills (seo-dataforseo, seo-image-gen) mirror MCP-backed capabilities.

A named methodology (FLOW = Find -> Leverage -> Optimize -> Win, plus Local) encoded as a skill carrying 41 stage-specific evidence-led prompts; `/seo flow [stage]` loads the matching stage's prompts. Methodology baked into a skill as a reusable operating model (CC BY 4.0, attributed to the author's separate flow repo).

~50 Python execution scripts (page fetchers, HTML parsers, validate_url SSRF guard, google_report.py PDF builder) sit at the plugin root scripts/ dir and are the concrete execution layer skills shell out to via the Bash tool — agents reason, scripts do deterministic fetch/parse/render.

Pattern_raw_name: Cross-harness packaging (Claude Code / Cursor / Antigravity / Gemini CLI)
How_implemented: Same skill/agent files are auto-discovered differently per harness: Claude Code + Antigravity via .claude-plugin/plugin.json, Cursor reads the directory structure directly, Gemini CLI uses AGENTS.md as entry. Installed via marketplace, install.sh (mac/linux) or install.ps1 (windows) — no piped-exec; inspectable before run.

hooks/hooks.json registers a PostToolUse hook matching Edit|Write that runs node hooks/run-python-hook.js -> hooks/validate-schema.py on ${tool_input.file_path}, validating generated schema/JSON-LD files immediately after the agent writes them — a CI-style quality gate enforced inline at write time.

Every skill is a folder skills/<name>/SKILL.md with minimal Agent-Skills-spec frontmatter: name (must equal dir, 1-64 lowercase/hyphen), description (1-1024 chars, trigger-phrase-heavy for auto-invocation by description match), optional metadata.version. Descriptions are intentionally packed with literal trigger phrases ('this page isn't converting', 'my landing page sucks', 'Product Hunt') and explicit scope-boundary cross-refs ('For signup flows, see signup').

Pattern_raw_name: Optional skill subdirectories: references/ scripts/ assets/
How_implemented: AGENTS.md prescribes the bundled-resource layout: SKILL.md required and kept <500 lines, with details pushed to references/ (loaded on demand), runnable code in scripts/, and templates/data in assets/. SKILL.md body embeds reusable structures inline (e.g. product-marketing ships a full markdown document template in a code fence the agent fills out).

Pattern_raw_name: Methodology-encoded marketing/PM frameworks (JTBD Four Forces, ORB channels, value-prop ladder)
How_implemented: Skills bake named frameworks into the prompt: product-marketing encodes JTBD Four Forces (Push/Pull/Habit/Anxiety) and a direct/secondary/indirect competitor taxonomy; launch encodes the ORB (Owned/Rented/Borrowed) channel framework; cro runs a 7-dimension diagnostic ordered by impact (value-prop clarity → headline → CTA hierarchy → visual hierarchy → trust → objection handling → friction). cro is a diagnosis skill (analyze a URL and reason) rather than a doc generator.

Each SKILL.md ends with a 'Related Skills' section naming sibling skills for scope handoff (pricing → churn-prevention/cro/copywriting/marketing-psychology/ab-testing/revops/sales-enablement). The README renders this as an explicit dependency diagram with product-marketing as the always-first foundation and bidirectional edges (copywriting ↔ cro ↔ ab-testing). It's loose orchestration by reference rather than a hard router.

Pattern_raw_name: Interactive elicitation gate (task-specific questions before output)
How_implemented: Skills front-load a small clarifying-question gate. product-marketing offers two modes (auto-draft-from-codebase vs walk-through-from-scratch) and walks sections one at a time, pushing for verbatim customer language. pricing ends with 6 task-specific questions (current ARPU/conversion, value metric, self-serve vs sales-led) before recommending. cro asks page type / conversion goal / traffic context first.

product-marketing dictates the exact output file structure via a full markdown template (12 H2 sections with bold field labels and empty tables to fill). pricing ships before/after checklists (defined personas, value metric, WTP research, tier mapping) as a gating list. The agent fills a fixed skeleton rather than free-forming.

Pattern_raw_name: Skill quality validators — validate-skills.sh + validate-skills-official.sh + CI matrix
How_implemented: Two-layer validation. validate-skills.sh is a self-contained bash linter: extracts frontmatter with awk, regex-checks name format ^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]{0,62}[a-z0-9])?$ + dir-match, description length 1-1024, warns if description lacks trigger words (when/mention/use) or scope cross-ref (see/for/ref), flags top-level version that should be under metadata, and warns if SKILL.md >500 lines. validate-skills-official.sh clones agentskills/agentskills skills-ref and runs the official `skills-ref validate`. CI (.github/workflows/validate-skill.yml) detects changed SKILL.md dirs via git diff, fans them out as a build matrix, and runs the Flash-Brew-Digital/validate-skill@v1 action per skill.

Pattern_raw_name: Cross-harness packaging via Agent Skills spec + multiple installers
How_implemented: Skills install to the cross-agent .agents/skills/ standard with a .claude/skills/ symlink for Claude Code. Six install paths documented: npx skills (vercel-labs/skills, auto-symlinks), Claude Code /plugin marketplace, clone-and-copy, git submodule, fork, and npx skillkit (multi-agent: Claude/Cursor/Copilot). AGENTS.md explicitly fences Claude-Code-only syntax (!`command`) out of SKILL.md to preserve cross-agent portability.

Repo doubles as a Claude Code plugin marketplace: plugin.json (name marketing-skills, version 2.5.1, skills:./skills) and marketplace.json list the installable pack. README documents both auto-invoked skills (description match) AND direct slash-command invocation (/cro, /emails, /seo-audit) — the commands-vs-skills split. A sync-skills CI job (sync-skills.js) auto-regenerates marketplace.json/plugin.json/README skill table from the skills/ folder on every push, committed by Coreybot.

tools/clis/ holds 51 zero-dependency Node 18+ scripts (ga4.js, mixpanel.js, stripe, semrush, etc.) callable by agents. Each reads creds from env vars, supports a --dry-run flag that returns the would-be request (method/url/redacted-headers/body) without sending, and prints JSON. AGENTS.md gives a verify recipe (node --check, no-args=help, --dry-run preview). These are bundled runnable code the marketing skills reference for implementation.

Pattern_raw_name: Once-per-session skill self-update check via VERSIONS.md
How_implemented: AGENTS.md instructs the agent, on first skill use per session, to fetch VERSIONS.md from GitHub and compare against local skill versions; prompt only if >=2 skills changed or any major bump, as a non-blocking end-of-response note; on 'update skills' it runs git pull. Each skill carries metadata.version so drift is detectable.

market/SKILL.md is the single entry skill; its Routing Logic section parses `/market <command> <arg>` and routes to skills/market-<command>/SKILL.md. Note: SKILL.md files carry NO YAML frontmatter — they are plain Markdown system prompts; invocation is via the `/market` command convention + install into ~/.claude/skills/, not description-match auto-invocation. The orchestrator chains commands (audit feeds report/proposal; brand feeds copy; funnel feeds emails) via the shared output files.

Pattern_raw_name: market-audit orchestrator skill (Phase 1 Discovery / Phase 2 Parallel / Phase 3 Aggregate)
How_implemented: Three-phase workflow skill: Phase 1 Discovery (fetch + business-type detection + page map), Phase 2 'Launch all 5 subagents simultaneously using Claude Code's subagent capability', Phase 3 aggregate dimension scores into a weighted composite (Content 25% / Conversion 20% / SEO 20% / Competitive 15% / Brand 10% / Growth 10%) and emit MARKETING-AUDIT.md plus a terminal scorecard with ASCII bar gauges and letter grade. Enforced output contract: fixed report skeleton with Revenue Impact Summary table and Next Steps.

Skill instructs the model to gather the 9 audit Markdown files, marshal them into an EXACT JSON schema ({url,date,brand_name,overall_score,executive_summary,categories:{<cat>:{score,weight}},...}), write it to disk, then shell out to scripts/generate_pdf_report.py (reportlab) which renders a branded PDF with score gauges, vertical bar charts, comparison tables and a prioritized action plan using a fixed hex color palette. Deterministic chart rendering offloaded to bundled Python; LLM only produces the JSON.

Pattern_raw_name: 14 domain sub-skills (market-copy, market-emails, market-social, market-ads, market-funnel, market-competitors, market-landing, market-launch, market-proposal, market-report, market-seo, market-brand, ...)
How_implemented: Each marketing function is its own folder skills/market-<x>/SKILL.md with a named output artifact; templates/ and scripts/ (analyze_page.py, competitor_scanner.py, social_calendar.py) provide bundled resources several skills call. Methodology baked in: CRO funnels, AIDA-style copy, A/B-test hypothesis generation in 'If we [change] then [metric] because [reason]' format.

Folder skill: SKILL.md frontmatter (name + auto-invocation description naming triggers 'prioritizing features, synthesizing user research, writing requirement documentation'); scripts/ holds executable rice_prioritizer.py and customer_interview_analyzer.py; references/ has prd_templates.md (Standard/One-Page/Feature Brief/Agile Epic), frameworks.md, and input-output-examples.md (the method+template+example triad). Body routes three 6-phase workflows (Prioritization / Discovery / PRD). One skill spanning many PM sub-domains via internal routing; methodologies baked in: RICE, MoSCoW, Kano, JTBD, Opportunity Solution Tree.

argparse CLI: positional input (CSV path or 'sample' to self-generate test data), --capacity (person-months/quarter, default 10), --output {text,json,csv}. Maps categorical inputs to numeric via internal dicts (impact 'massive'=3.0 … 'minimal'=0.25), computes rice_score=(reach*impact*confidence)/effort, then portfolio-classifies: quick wins = impact in [massive,high] AND effort in [xs,s]; big bets = impact in [massive,high] AND effort in [l,xl]; emits ranked features + analysis object + quarterly roadmap. Stdlib-only, deterministic, no LLM calls — passes --help/--sample smoke tests per repo contract.

A reasoning/diagnosis skill (not just doc-gen). Outcome-based gating: must 'Define desired outcome' (one measurable outcome + baseline + target) before proceeding; requires ≥3 distinct opportunities before converging; mandates sequential artifacts (measurable outcome → Opportunity Solution Tree → assumption-prioritization matrix → evidence synthesis → proceed/pivot/stop decision gates). Methodology-encoded: Teresa Torres' Opportunity Solution Tree, four-dimension assumption mapping (desirability/viability/feasibility/usability), evidence triangulation ('Tie every branch to evidence source').

A binding authoring contract: each skill = SKILL.md (≤10KB) + references/ + templates/ + scripts/; required SKILL.md sections (YAML frontmatter, practitioner opener, Before Starting, How This Skill Works multi-mode, Core Content, 4-6 Proactive Triggers, Output Artifacts table, Related Skills WHEN/NOT disambiguation, Communication Standard). Enforced by CI: scripts/audit_skills.py (runs every SKILL.md through a checklist runner → PASS/WARN/FAIL), derive_counters.py --check (docs vs actual tree counts), check_plugin_json.py --all (validates ./-prefixed skills paths). 'Matt's 6-item checklist' (decision triggers, anti-patterns, success criteria, references, templates, forcing questions) is a post-v2.6.0 binding gate.

A documented skills-generator path: a megaprompt body becomes SKILL.md verbatim, then a wrapper auto-adds validators, 3 reference docs (5-7 sources each), a cs-* agent, a /cs:* command, and a plugin.json 'source' field documenting the spec+build provenance. Codifies turning raw expert prompts into the full 11-file skill contract — a meta-skill that manufactures skills.

Pattern_raw_name: cs-* agent + /cs:* command pairing per skill
How_implemented: Each skill ships a persona entry point: a cs-<name> agent (markdown+frontmatter) AND a /cs:<name> slash command, giving an explicit user-invoked command surface distinct from auto-invoked skills. The cs- prefix is applied only for ClawHub slug conflicts, not folder names. 99 slash commands / 93 agents across the repo.

Root carries parallel adapter trees (.codex-plugin/, .codex/, .gemini/, .hermes/skills/claude-skills/, .vibe/skills/claude-skills/, custom-gpt/) so one skill body runs across 13 claimed platforms. .claude-plugin/marketplace.json registers 78 plugins (name/source ./path/description/version/author/keywords/category) installable via '/plugin marketplace add alirezarezvani/claude-skills'. INSTALLATION.md + scripts/ provide install/validate tooling.

Captures patterns to MEMORY.md without judgment during sessions; /si:review adds curation judgment (promotion-readiness, consolidation); /si:promote graduates recurring patterns to CLAUDE.md/rules/. A skill-extractor agent isolates proven patterns into standalone properly-structured skills (SKILL.md + docs) only after they prove out — a quarantine-until-mature gate preventing immature patterns becoming reusable infrastructure. No taste-profile mechanism; promotion is human-gated.

Single skill = folder with SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter: name, description with explicit trigger phrases + '/create-second-brain-prd', argument-hint: <path-to-requirements> [output-path]), a requirements template, a worked example template, and references/architecture-reference.md loaded just-in-time. It's a meta-skill / skills-generator: it reads a filled requirements template + the architecture blueprint, web-researches every chosen platform's API/auth/limits, then emits a personalized PRD that itself instructs building further project-specific skills. References ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} to locate its bundled files.

An 8-section fill-in template (About You / Platforms / Top Tasks for AI / Proactivity Level / Security Boundaries / Memory Categories / Infrastructure / Integration Priority) used as a structured elicitation gate before generation, with Proactivity Level offering a calibrated 4-tier menu (Observer notify-only → Advisor draft-for-review → Assistant act-on-low-risk → Partner autonomous). A separate example-* file is the few-shot worked sample. Method-file + template-file + example-file triad.

The blueprint prescribes the standard skill folder shape the generated brain should use — SKILL.md + scripts/ (deterministic local logic) + references/ (on-demand docs) + assets/ — with three load tiers: metadata always (~100 words frontmatter), body on trigger, resources on demand. This is described/prescribed, not shipped as runnable skills in this repo.

One top-level SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description) defines the whole system: three principles (AI-First Vault Rule, Bi-Temporal Facts, Living System Architecture) + propagation matrix + two-output rule, dispatching to 44 commands and bundled references/ (ai-first-rules.md, write-rules.md). One skill operates across people/projects/tasks/decisions/research/thinking domains via internal command routing.

Commands are individual .md files with frontmatter `description / category / triggers_en: [natural-language phrases]`. Grouped into Layer 1 Operations (28), Layer 2 Thinking Tools (8), Layer 3 Context Engine (1: /obsidian-world), Layer 4 Research (7). Workflow commands (/obsidian-save, /obsidian-ingest) chain into component subagents; explicit commands-as-user-entrypoints split.

Non-negotiable note contract: every note must have a `## For future Claude` 2-3 sentence preamble immediately after frontmatter; universal frontmatter (date YYYY-MM-DD, type, tags incl. type, ai-first: true) plus type-specific fields (Project/Person/Task/Decision/Meeting schemas); inline recency markers; confidence levels; mandatory wikilinks; ASCII-only (no smart quotes/em-dashes). An explicit anti-pattern table (date: today, bare claims, omitted URLs, 'see above') makes violations checkable.

A PostToolUse-style bash hook lints every written note against ai-first-rules.md: (1) frontmatter delimiters present, (2) no tabs in YAML, (3) required fields date/type/tags/ai-first:true, (4) body contains `## For future Claude` ('rule #2'), (5) banned-unicode scan (Python allowlist mapping each char to ASCII replacement). Exit 1 + stderr warning on failure; writes never reverted (advisory lint, not a gate).

Three hard rules baked into every command: False Absence (never claim a note/person doesn't exist without exhaustive search across aliases), Search Completeness (enumerate, don't sample; report only what was searched), No Fabrication (mark unknowns TBD; never invent facts/dates/rates/relationships to complete a section). 'Search first' precedes every create to prevent duplicates.

One source (commands/, references/, SKILL.md) compiles to 6 runtimes via adapters/{claude-code,codex-cli,gemini-cli,opencode,pi,hermes}/ + shared adapters/lib.sh. `bash scripts/build.sh --platform <target>` emits a platform-appropriate dispatcher (CLAUDE.md / GEMINI.md+.gemini/commands/ / AGENTS.md+.opencode/commands/ / .agents/skills/ for Codex / .pi/prompts/+.pi/skills/ / native Hermes skills). One-line curl quick-install.sh + /obsidian-init bootstrap.

Layer 2 reasoning commands (not doc-generators): /obsidian-challenge stress-tests an idea against the user's own vault history, /obsidian-emerge surfaces unnamed patterns, /obsidian-connect bridges unrelated domains, /obsidian-graduate promotes an idea to a project, /vault-deep-synthesis + /obsidian-distill compress. Distinct from capture/ingest commands — they reason over the existing KB.

Pattern_raw_name: Research toolkit with graceful key-less degradation (/research, /x-read, /youtube, /podcast)
How_implemented: Layer 4 ingests live data (Grok X posts, Perplexity web, YouTube transcripts, podcast parsing, NotebookLM) keyed via ~/.config/obsidian-second-brain/.env; /research degrades gracefully to free key-less sources (Wikipedia, HackerNews, arXiv, Reddit) when unkeyed. Findings land as AI-first notes with recency markers + source citations, then propagate (two-output rule).

/create-command generates new vault commands (project-specific skill generation) and /obsidian-architect / /obsidian-init scaffold the vault structure — meta-skills that produce more skills/structure rather than content.

Pattern_raw_name: Frontmatter-trigger skills discovered + routed at runtime (managed-table routing retired in v0.36)
How_implemented: Each skill is skills/<slug>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter: name, version, description, triggers: (array of user-facing phrases), tools: (allow-list), mutating:, writes_pages:, writes_to:, brain_first:. _AGENT_README.md instructs the agent to DISCOVER skills at runtime by walking every skills/*/SKILL.md and parsing frontmatter, then substring-match (optionally + embedding similarity) the user message against every triggers array. Explicitly 'Do NOT look for a managed-block table in RESOLVER.md — that pattern was retired in v0.36; routing lives in frontmatter now.' Optional routing-eval.jsonl per skill tests dispatch accuracy.

Pattern_raw_name: RESOLVER.md thin dispatcher + conventions/ deferral layer
How_implemented: skills/RESOLVER.md is a human-readable dispatcher mapping trigger phrases -> skill files, grouped by domain (brain ops, ingestion, thinking, operational, setup), with explicit disambiguation rules ('prefer most specific: meeting-ingestion over ingest', 'route URL by content type', 'chaining is explicit in each skill's Phases'). A skills/conventions/ folder holds cross-cutting rules (quality.md, brain-first.md, subagent-routing.md, schema-evolution.md, salience-and-recency.md) that EVERY brain-writing skill defers to via inline '> Convention:' callouts — DRY'd shared method so individual skills stay thin.

Pattern_raw_name: Enforced output-contract skills (Contract / Phases / Anti-Patterns / Output Format / Quality Rules)
How_implemented: Every SKILL.md follows a rigid body template: ## Contract ('this skill guarantees: ...'), ## Phases (numbered workflow), ## Anti-Patterns (named failure modes), ## Output Format (literal template), ## Quality Rules. query/SKILL.md's contract bans hallucination + mandates gap flags; signal-detector mandates exact-phrasing capture + back-links; maintain's output is a fixed per-dimension health table. The contracts are machine-checkable: skillpack-check audits conformance, and skillopt judges enforce required sections/citations.

Pattern_raw_name: Always-on ambient signal-detector skill (capture loop on every message)
How_implemented: skills/signal-detector/SKILL.md fires on EVERY inbound message (triggers: 'every inbound message (always-on)'), spawned as a cheap parallel subagent that never blocks the main reply. It captures TWO things at equal priority — original thinking (verbatim, no paraphrase, -> originals/{slug}) and entity mentions (-> people/companies, enrich if thin) — relies on the put_page auto-link hook, adds dated timeline entries for new facts, and emits a one-line debuggable signal log ('Signals: 1 idea, 2 entities, 0 facts'). This is the brain's continuous capture loop.

Pattern_raw_name: Self-improving skill-optimizer (SkillOpt: SKILL.md as trainable parameters)
How_implemented: skills/skill-optimizer/SKILL.md treats a SKILL.md body as the trainable parameters of a frozen agent (grounded in the SkillOpt paper). `gbrain skillopt <skill>` runs forward rollouts on a benchmark, 'backward' reflection over failures+successes, ranks/clips edits via an LR cosine schedule, and applies BODY-ONLY edits (frontmatter/triggers are invariant). A validation gate (median-of-3 + epsilon 0.05 margin on a held-out sel-set) is MANDATORY before any rewrite; cost preflight + per-skill DB lock + atomic versioned snapshots (skillopt/versions/, history.json, rejected.json LRU, ISO-week audit trail). Bundled skills are quarantined: in-place mutation requires BOTH --allow-mutate-bundled AND a >=5-task benchmark-disjoint --held-out set, else it hard-refuses and writes proposed.md for human review. Can bootstrap a starter benchmark from the SKILL.md itself.

A family of skills that operate ON skills: skill-creator + skillify scaffold/normalize a new SKILL.md; skillpack-check audits conformance (counts, triggers, cross-refs, skillopt status); skillpack-harvest 'lifts' a locally-authored skill upstream into gbrain so other clients inherit it; testing/SKILL.md validates the whole skill pack. functional-area-resolver compresses an overgrown RESOLVER.md/AGENTS.md routing surface into a functional-area dispatcher.

Pattern_raw_name: Skillpack-as-scaffolding: gbrain becomes a reference library, not law, after install
How_implemented: Skills are scaffolded into the host repo; on upgrade they DO NOT change automatically. `gbrain skillpack reference --all` sweeps every bundled skill and reports identical/differs/missing; per-skill `gbrain skillpack reference <slug>` prints a unified diff between gbrain's bundle and the local file; --apply-clean-hunks does a documented two-way merge (with a loud warning that it can't see the originally-scaffolded version). New bundled skills are pulled with `gbrain skillpack scaffold <new-slug>`. The contract: 'gbrain is reference, not law' — intentional local edits are kept. openclaw.plugin.json declares the skill list, shared_deps (conventions + _ rules), and excluded_from_install (setup/migrate/publish).

Pattern_raw_name: Declarative skill capabilities: tools allow-list, mutating, brain_first opt-out
How_implemented: Frontmatter declares the skill's capability surface machine-readably: tools: (e.g. submit_job/get_job for minion-orchestrator; search/query/get_page for query), mutating: true/false, writes_pages/writes_to: (sanctioned write dirs cross-checked against _brain-filing-rules.md). 'brain_first: exempt' is a STRICT canonical opt-out for pure-infra skills (cron/container/ask-user) whose parser refuses to guess on typos ('brain-first:'/'Exempt'/quoted forms print a paste-ready doctor fix and stay flagged). gbrain doctor --fix auto-adds the brain-first Convention callout, skipping opted-out skills.

Pattern_raw_name: Thinking-skill catalog (GStack) layered over the brain (chaining + brain-only fallback)
How_implemented: RESOLVER.md routes 'brainstorm/office-hours', 'CEO review/poke holes', 'investigate/debug', 'retro' to GStack thinking skills — a companion pack ('GStack teaches agents to code; GBrain teaches everything else'). If GStack is installed the agent reads them directly; if not, 'brain-only mode still works'. Concept/idea reasoning skills exist natively too: concept-synthesis (find patterns across notes), idea-lineage (trace how thinking on X evolved, show reversals), strategic-reading (read a source through the lens of a problem), book-mirror (two-column personalized book analysis).

Each skill is a top-level folder (qa/, ship/, review/, learn/, office-hours/...) holding a SKILL.md GENERATED from a SKILL.md.tmpl via 'bun run gen:skill-docs' (template + generated file both committed; merge conflicts on generated files must never be resolved by accepting a side). Frontmatter fields: name, preamble-tier (1-4), version (semver), description, triggers (list of natural-language phrases for auto-invocation), allowed-tools (least-privilege list e.g. Bash/Read/Write/Edit/AskUserQuestion/Glob/Grep). Generated SKILL.md trips a warning above 160KB (~40K tokens). Slash commands are the user-invoked surface; triggers drive auto-invocation.

A skill folder bundles SKILL.md, SKILL.md.tmpl, and a sections/ subdir of method/structure files the skill body references (office-hours has sections/design-and-handoff.md carrying the two design-doc templates — Startup mode vs Builder mode — and the handoff/quality-gate logic). Heavy procedural detail lives in sections rather than bloating SKILL.md.

Pattern_raw_name: /autoplan — command chaining four review skills into one pipeline
How_implemented: /autoplan READS the SKILL.md files of plan-ceo-review, plan-design-review, plan-eng-review, plan-devex-review FROM DISK and executes them in strict order (CEO -> Design[if UI] -> Eng -> DX[if dev-facing]), resolving each phase's AskUserQuestion calls itself via six auto-decide principles instead of prompting the user. Classifies every decision Mechanical (silent) / Taste (auto+surface at gate) / User-Challenge (never auto-decided). One entry command orchestrates the whole plan-review workflow and recommends /ship next.

Pattern_raw_name: /office-hours — interactive elicitation gate (YC six forcing questions)
How_implemented: Phased diagnostic: Phase1 context gather, Phase2A/2B mode-specific questioning (startup = six forcing questions on demand reality/status-quo/specificity/wedge/observation/future-fit; builder = generative), Phase3 mandatory Premise Challenge, Phase4 mandatory 2-3 alternatives chosen by user via AskUserQuestion. Hard rule: never batch questions into one AskUserQuestion (ask one, wait), never skip Premise/Alternatives even for 'obvious' plans, no soft language ('that's interesting' forbidden in startup mode), no design doc until user approves the chosen approach. The named YC methodology is baked in.

Pattern_raw_name: /design-shotgun taste profiles — self-improving with confidence decay
How_implemented: Stores approved+rejected design choices per dimension (fonts/colors/layouts/aesthetics) in ~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG/taste-profile.json (schema v1), each entry recording value, confidence, approve/reject count, last-seen. Confidence DECAYS 5%/week of inactivity, computed at READ time (file grows only on change). bin/gstack-taste-update approved|rejected <variant-path> writes choices, handles schema migration from legacy approved.json, and FLAGS conflicts ('your profile strongly prefers minimal, you're asking for playful — proceed or update?'). Learns after 3+ feedback rounds; biases future variant generation without explicit instruction.

Pattern_raw_name: Skill quarantine + auto-activation after 3 successful uses (README feature)
How_implemented: Per the README and CLAUDE.md proactive system: newly discovered domain skills are QUARANTINED and only auto-activate after 3 successful uses, with optional promotion to the global allowlist; proactive skill suggestions are tied to sprint stage (brainstorming/reviewing/debugging/testing). The user-facing gating prompts live in preamble generators (generate-proactive-prompt.ts, gated on PROACTIVE_PROMPTED/TEL_PROMPTED, writes ~/.gstack/.proactive-prompted, toggled via gstack-config set proactive). NOTE: the exact counting/promotion code file was not pinned during this dive — the threshold logic is documented but its implementing binary was not located.

Pattern_raw_name: Template-generated docs + enforced output contracts (Diataxis, design-doc templates)
How_implemented: SKILL.md files are auto-generated from .tmpl, and /document-generate emits Diataxis-framework docs (reference/how-to/tutorial/explanation). Skills enforce output contracts: office-hours' design doc must contain a fixed section set (Problem Statement, Demand Evidence, Wedge, Premises, Approaches Considered, Success Criteria...) and a spec-review subagent scores it 1-10 on completeness/consistency/clarity/scope/feasibility, re-dispatching until PASS (max 3) — placeholder-proof gate. /autoplan mandates 'artifacts must exist on disk, not summaries'.

Pattern_raw_name: Cross-host packaging + CLI installer (./setup, --host, team mode)
How_implemented: One git-clone + ./setup install into ~/.claude/skills/gstack. --host flag (or auto-detect) targets Claude Code / Codex CLI / OpenCode / Cursor / Factory Droid / Kiro / Hermes; agents/openai.yaml carries the Codex adapter. Team mode (setup --team + gstack-team-init) auto-updates shared repos. Windows uses file copies vs symlinks. Per-skill allowed-tools enforce least-privilege.

~87 user-invoked /gsd:* slash commands (plan-phase, execute-phase, spec-phase, validate-phase, secure-phase, ui-phase, map-codebase, ingest-docs, graphify, extract-learnings, etc.), each a markdown file with YAML frontmatter declaring name, description, argument-hint (full flag grammar like '[phase] [--auto] [--research] [--prd <file>]'), allowed-tools (incl. the Agent tool), and a requires: [..] dependency list on other commands. The command body is an orchestration script that chains subagents — an explicit commands(user)-vs-agents(spawned) split.

Planner encodes a named method: derive must-haves by working backward from the phase outcome (observable truths → required artifacts → critical wiring connections), then build a task dependency graph grouped into parallel waves (Wave 1 = zero-dependency tasks). Enforced output contract: each task must carry type, files, action, Verify command, and acceptance criteria; every locked decision (D-01, D-02…) must trace to ≥1 task; scope reduction is forbidden unless context budget/missing-info/dependency conflict forces a phase split.

Research skill tags every claim with confidence provenance: [VERIFIED: source] / [CITED: docs.url] / [ASSUMED], with a tool priority hierarchy (Context7 HIGH > WebFetch MEDIUM > WebSearch/Exa/Firecrawl conditional). A 'Package Legitimacy Audit' (slopcheck) marks any package found outside official docs as [ASSUMED] regardless of registry confirmation, flagging it for user validation before decisions lock — an anti-hallucination gate for dependency selection.

Pattern_raw_name: Cross-runtime command packaging + bin/ CLI installer
How_implemented: Same command/agent markdown bodies are packaged across runtimes (Grok Build, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex); hooks have per-runtime event mapping (PostToolUse vs Gemini AfterTool). bin/ holds the CLI entry points and get-shit-done/ is the installable package; the SDK assembles prompts per runtime. npm-distributed.

Pattern_raw_name: 19-skill folder set with SKILL.md + YAML frontmatter
How_implemented: Each skill is a folder .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with frontmatter name + description + roles:[...] + integrations:[...]; model-invoked via description match (plugin.json sets invocation:'model-invoked', progressiveDisclosure:true). 19 skills span personal-KM (braindump, daily-brief, weekly-checkin, url-dump, scout), team-intel (team-brief, meeting-transcript, comprehensive-analysis), PM-chain (auto-research, generate-prd, create-user-story, generate-release-notes, update-knowledge-base, export-open-issues, publish-to-confluence), and meta (onboarding, update-cog, knowledge-consolidation, loop-engineering).

A skill that exists to be CITED by other iterative skills, not run standalone. Defines a 6-step loop (Gather->Act->Observe->Verify->Update->Decide) where Verify is non-negotiable ('trust deterministic checks, never the agent's looks-done self-report'), LAYERED termination (deterministic verifier + hard iteration cap + budget guard + no-progress detection + human escalation), verification principles (prefer mechanical date/field/count checks), and a named-pattern table (Act-observe/ReAct, Reflect-retry/Reflexion, Plan-execute-verify, Evaluator-optimizer, Orchestrator-workers, Loop-until-dry, Human-in-the-loop). knowledge-consolidation, daily-brief, team-brief inherit this.

Decomposes a question into 5-7 independent research threads (market forces, historical precedent, player analysis, tech trajectories, customer behavior, economics, contrarian/emerging-tech). Thread 7 always mines pre-mainstream sources (GitHub repos, arXiv, Twitter builders, Discord, conference talks). agent_mode-aware: team mode launches all worker-researcher agents in one message with run_in_background:true (8-12 searches each); solo mode runs 2-3 sequential passes. No hallucinated sources; explicit confidence + gaps; output archived to 05-knowledge/research/.

Pre-flight asserts MY-PROFILE.md exists (else onboarding) and runs `date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M'` via Bash for a real (never estimated) timestamp. 5-phase analysis (ingestion->structural->domain-classification-with-confidence%->insight-extraction->competitive-intel). Routes output by domain: personal->02-, professional->03-, project->04-projects/<slug>/braindumps/, mixed->00-inbox/. If watchlist company mentioned, auto-creates/updates 04-projects/<project>/competitive/<company>.md linked back to the braindump. Enforces Obsidian-valid YAML.

Pattern_raw_name: team-brief 6-source cross-reference skill with Linear sync-back
How_implemented: 4-phase: setup (Mon=Fri-Sun lookback else yesterday) -> 6 parallel subagents (github-analyst, slack-monitor, meeting-reviewer, linear-tracker, braindump-reviewer, posthog-analyst) -> orchestrator runs 23-point linkage logic (Linear<->PR mismatch flags, PostHog spike<->merge correlation, meeting-action<->actual-work follow-up) with a 10-level alert priority ranking -> WRITE-BACK to Linear via API (attach PR URLs, auto-transition states, post initiative status) -> HackMD publish -> Slack preview requiring user confirmation. Solo mode skips sync-back/publish. Never fabricates; duplicate-prevention before linking.

Reads MY-INTEGRATIONS.md (publish targets) + MY-PROFILE.md (projects/role/agent_mode), elicits feature/problem/users/solution, emits a fixed 9-section PRD, writes to 04-projects/<project>/PRDs/, then a hard approval gate before publishing to Confluence/Notion/HackMD ('never auto-publishes'). Frontmatter declares roles:[product-manager,engineering-lead,founder] + integrations:[confluence,notion,hackmd] so role packs can recommend it.

Same surface re-emitted per runtime: .claude/skills (19 full), .kiro/powers (7 POWER.md core), .gemini/commands (7) + .gemini/skills playbooks, AGENTS.md (universal markdown fallback documenting all skills with triggers+steps+output paths for Codex/any markdown agent), .cursorrules. plugin.json metadata.nativeSurfaces records the per-harness counts. AGENTS.md also standardizes worker /tmp file exchange + config-file system so non-Claude agents get the same behavior.

scripts/validate-agent-surface.sh: validates plugin.json + marketplace-entry.json JSON; asserts skill count in manifest == actual SKILL.md files; asserts every declared skill is documented in AGENTS.md; checks manifest paths exist; enforces version PARITY across plugin.json, marketplace-entry.json and COG-VERSION; per-harness count gates (Kiro=7 POWER.md, Gemini=7 commands+7 skills); doc-capitalization + docs/AGENT-SUPPORT.md existence. Exit 1 on error, warnings non-fatal. Run before publish / after framework updates.

Checks for and applies upstream COG framework updates (cog-update.sh / COG-VERSION) without touching personal content — framework files (.claude/.kiro/.gemini) refresh while user vault dirs (00-05) are preserved. Maintains the 'self-evolving / self-healing with auto-updating cross-references' claim in plugin.json features.

SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter; Step 0 routes intent into init/append/query/deep (default query when ambiguous; ingest is opt-in via explicit verbs/file drops). Step 0.5 does CLI preflight (obsidian/readwise/nlm/git), hard-stopping only if discovery mode + all discovery CLIs missing + no seeds. It is the dispatcher that spawns 8 specialist subagents and calls bundled Python scripts. Folder bundles agents/ + scripts/ + CONVENTIONS.md.

Deep mode exposes three configurable presets controlling research-round budget: fast = 1 round x 3 queries; light = 2 rounds (3+2); deep = 3 rounds x 3. Quantified runtime estimates per mode (seconds to 40+ min). Lets the user trade cost/latency vs coverage explicitly.

Compresses a research dir down to a single research.md containing ONLY sources actually used in a target content file (article/outline/guidelines): matches each source to the content by explicit reference (title/author/URL/citation #) or traceable ideas (distinctive framings/terms), then distills matched sources into guideline-relative summaries (relevant claims + <=3 verbatim quotes + wiki pointers + full URI metadata), ordered by relevance, one <details> block per source, with a token-budget estimate for the downstream writer agent's context window.

Quality validator for the KB (see CM-16): 7 checks (4 script-mechanical + 3 LLM), parallel execution (cheap mechanical first, then 3 LLM subagents in parallel), capped reporting (top-5 shown, full lists in JSON scratch), log + index regeneration. A diagnosis/health skill rather than doc-gen.

Transforms wiki pages (sources/entities/concepts/synthesis only — never raw) into marp slide decks, matplotlib charts (mandatory reproducible .py + PNG), Obsidian Canvas JSON argument maps, or social briefs. Spawns format-specific writer subagents in parallel; charts execute the generated .py to produce the PNG (executable skill). Renders file back into wiki/renders/ to compound. Idempotency check skips when output exists with identical prompt+sources.

Pattern_raw_name: Source CLI helper skills (obsidian-cli, readwise-cli, nlm-skill)
How_implemented: Three thin wrapper skills expose each external knowledge source (Obsidian vault search, Readwise library+feed, NotebookLM) as a uniform CLI that researcher subagents call; the orchestrator preflight-checks them and degrades gracefully (warn + skip a missing source rather than fail).

Bundled scripts github_parse_targets.py + github_clone.py parse repo-relative file refs from the brain dump, group them into 'modules' by directory, shallow-clone to a sibling .github-cache/ (outside the research dir), then spawn one source_writer per module in parallel plus one github_spec_writer that emits a single ARCHITECTURE.md whose Module Index uses inline relative links (module docs are NOT separate index entries). One seed entry per repo keyed to ARCHITECTURE.md.

Pattern_raw_name: skills/ folder structure (SKILL.md + eval.json + learnings.md + context/)
How_implemented: Each of 16 skills is a folder with: SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter name/description/version/author/tags + sections Description/When to Use/Input/Process/Output/Agent Assignment/Dependencies), eval.json (weighted scoring criteria), learnings.md (accumulated insights), and an ephemeral context/ folder for skill-specific scratch data. The eval+learnings pair makes skills self-improving: every run updates learnings and score-history, consolidated weekly.

skills/_template/SKILL.md is a fixed authoring contract: enforced frontmatter (name/description/version/author/tags) and required sections (Description, When to Use, Input, Process, Output, Agent Assignment primary+fallback, Dependencies). New skills are cloned from it, and an auto-skill generator turns a natural-language description into this complete structure.

Each skill ships an eval.json defining weighted success criteria (e.g. heartbeat: alerts_caught 0.4, all_agents_online 0.3, response_time_under_5s 0.3). Runs produce a score-history that consolidation prunes to the latest 20 — a per-skill quality/contract layer that scores outputs rather than just defining them.

Every SKILL.md declares Primary + Fallback agent among opencode/hermes/gemini (e.g. research-synthesis -> gemini primary, opencode fallback for formatting; project-planner -> opencode/gemini; memory-consolidation -> hermes/opencode). This wires skill -> runtime dispatch and gives graceful degradation when an agent is offline.

Six-step methodology-encoded research skill: define questions -> multi-platform web search via Gemini CLI -> isolate key info per source -> cross-verify across sources -> consolidate -> attribute citations. Output contract: exec summary + detailed findings + source citations + recommendations. Provenance/citation requirement is baked into the output format.

Converts an objective into a spec doc (UIs + data structures), then decomposes into 2-5 minute increments, documenting each task with destination filepath + code scaffold + validation procedure, and archives spec+plan in context/. Reads standards/ for conventions first. Spec-as-source-of-truth feeding executable micro-tasks.

Reads active-projects.md + last-24h audit log + recent-decisions.md to synthesize a morning briefing (yesterday's accomplishments / today's objectives / blockers). A pure context-aggregation skill assigned to hermes, fired by cron.

Five-step pipeline: read cost-history.json -> aggregate daily tokens per agent -> project weekly/monthly spend -> compare to free-tier limits in settings.json -> alert at 80/90/95% and refresh the dashboard chart dataset. Enforces the constitution's Cost Consciousness principle programmatically (all three agents default to zero-cost models).

43 folder-per-skill units each with a SKILL.md carrying minimal YAML frontmatter (name + multi-line description only). Description-match auto-invocation. Organized by domain cluster: agentdb-* (memory/vector), swarm-*/hive-mind-advanced (coordination), github-* (repo ops), sparc-methodology, v3-* (architecture migration), verification-quality. Slash commands in .claude/commands chain skills into workflows.

A skills-generator skill: teaches Claude to author new SKILL.md skills with valid frontmatter (name ≤64ch, description ≤1024ch with explicit what+when clauses), flat directory rules (no nested subdirs; optional scripts/resources/docs), and a 3-level progressive-disclosure budget (L1 metadata ~200 chars at startup, L2 SKILL.md body ~1-10KB on trigger, L3 referenced files on demand) so 100+ skills coexist with minimal context cost.

Encodes the swarm methodology as a skill: 3-tier queen hierarchy (strategic/tactical/adaptive), 8 worker types with keyword+performance-based task assignment, 3 consensus algorithms (majority / weighted where queen vote=3x / Byzantine 2/3), and an 8-type collective memory model (knowledge/context/task/result/error/metric/consensus/system) over SQLite WAL + LRU cache. Primary interface is npx claude-flow hive-mind CLI rather than MCP tools.
SPARC (Specification-Pseudocode-Architecture-Refinement-Completion) baked in as a named methodology skill with 18 specialized modes, surfaced via sparc.md command and a commands/sparc dir.

SKILL.md with name+description frontmatter scoped tightly ('Do NOT use for content generation or non-vault file operations'). Bundles executable Python (scripts/graph.py, swarm_prepare.py, swarm_reduce.py) the skill calls via uv run. Acts as the engine other skills depend on (dbrain-processor depends_on: [autograph]). Five named workflows (BOOTSTRAP/HEALTH/CREATE/SEARCH&LINK/ORCHESTRATE) the agent routes between. Hard self-corrections baked in: 'Use only swarm-links, never links — 81.6% vs 0.3% match'.

10-phase orchestrating skill (Capture->Execute->Reflect). frontmatter declares depends_on: [autograph]. Three-way classification (task / thought / business-mention) using GTD-flavored rules; calls autograph to mint typed cards; enforces a strict output contract: 'ALWAYS return RAW HTML. No markdown. Ever.', <=4096 chars, allowlist of 6 Telegram-safe tags. Idempotency gate: if entries pre-marked <!-- ✓ processed --> it returns a simplified status template instead of reprocessing.

Natural-language scheduling skill (RU+EN triggers). Translates 'remind me every morning' into a jobs.json entry via a CLI supporting three modes: --cron "0 9 * * *" --tz, --every 3600 (interval), --at ISO --delete-after-run (one-shot). Hard rule: 'Never emulate scheduling through sleep/loops/background processes — use only this CLI.' A host poller checks jobs.json every minute.

Diagnosis/repair skill (not doc-generation). Classifies an install as v1/v2/v3.0, identifies WHY upgrade.sh fails (dirty git, legacy systemd units, missing auth, wedged tmux session), removes the blocking obstacle, reruns the canonical upgrade.sh rather than reimplementing migration. Safeguard: never touches vault content, only code/units/runtime. Success = active systemd units + doctor service ok=True.

Pattern_raw_name: SKILL.md anatomy (Trigger / Context / Instructions / Constraints / Output Format / Examples / Anti-patterns)
How_implemented: Each skill is a single SKILL.md using MARKDOWN H2 SECTION HEADERS, not YAML frontmatter (confirmed: files begin with '# <Skill Name>' then '## Trigger' / '## Behavior' — no name/description frontmatter block exists). The canonical template (templates/SKILL-TEMPLATE.md) defines the contract: Trigger (with explicit keyword list), Context, numbered Instructions with embedded decision logic ('If user provides X, do Y, otherwise ask'), Constraints, Output Format (with a fenced structure skeleton), 2 Examples (User says / Claude produces), Anti-patterns ('DO NOT…'). Enforced output: skills mandate exact structures — status-update-writer says 'Follow this exact structure' with a fixed TL;DR/Status/sections layout; idea-validator emits required scorecard tables and a GO/ITERATE/STOP verdict.

Most skills open with a 'Step 1: Understand/Gather' block that asks 3-4 clarifying questions BEFORE producing output (idea-validator: idea-in-one-sentence / who specifically / current solution / why you; status-update-writer: project / what happened / audience / is-there-bad-news; linkedin-post-writer: core insight / audience / hook / format). idea-validator additionally codes a pushback rule: 'If the user gives a vague answer to #2 (e.g. everyone), push back. Every viable idea has a specific first customer.'

idea-validator bakes in a 5-dimension rubric (Problem Severity, Market Evidence, Solution Differentiation, Feasibility, Business Viability) each with a Strong/Moderate/Weak scoring GUIDE giving concrete thresholds (e.g. '$1M ARR requires <1,000 customers = Strong'), plus a competitive-scan sub-step and a graveyard check. prompt-engineer encodes named prompt techniques (Role Priming, Structured Output, Chain of Thought) with weak/better/best ladders. These are reasoning/diagnosis skills, not just doc generators.

Pattern_raw_name: Good-vs-bad paired worked examples + gap markers
How_implemented: idea-validator ships a 'Good vs. Bad Validation Examples' section showing the SAME idea done well and done poorly (AI sales note-taker; dog-owner social network), each annotated with WHY the bad one fails ('gives ratings without evidence… worthless'). This contrastive few-shot teaches the discrimination, not just the target. Anti-hallucination: skills require '[NEED: competitive research on X]' markers when data is missing and 'ASSUMPTION:' labels for guesses, and cap Market Evidence at Moderate if the scan can't be grounded.

AGENTS.md states 'Custom skills are available in .claude/skills/. They auto-trigger when relevant or can be invoked directly with /skill-name' — the standard Claude Code SKILL.md + frontmatter model. The public repo ships the convention/hook for skills more than a large library of them; the dominant 'skill' here is the inlined backlog/goals method baked directly into AGENTS.md.

Each integration under core/integrations/<name>/ ships a triad: README.md + SETUP_SKILL.md + mcp-config.json. SETUP_SKILL.md is a skill the user tells Claude to follow ('set up the Granola integration') and the agent executes the install/config steps and wires the MCP server from the mcp-config.json template — a skill-as-installer pattern for optional add-ons.

examples/workflows/*.md encode named repeatable PM routines as numbered-step prompts the agent runs against the file tree: weekly-review aggregates done tasks, compares progress to GOALS.md quarterly goals, finds blocked tasks, re-plans, and updates GOALS.md if priorities shifted. They are method recipes that chain the MCP tools rather than auto-invoked SKILL.md skills.

The flagship executable skill, implemented in core/mcp/server.py as an MCP tool: SequenceMatcher similarity on lowercased titles combined as title_sim*0.7 + keyword_overlap*0.3 (keywords = item text minus stop-words/short terms); >=0.6 flags a likely duplicate (>0.8 → recommend merge, else review). Separately, regex over vague verbs ('fix','update') flags ambiguous items and generates clarification questions — the agent must STOP and ask before creating an under-specified task.

The AGENTS.md backlog flow mandates a full YAML+sections task file with complete metadata (priority, category, status, goal citation in Context) before a task is considered created; missing context/priority/next-step is a hard stop. config.yaml constrains the value space (6 categories, 4 priorities, 4 statuses) and auto-categorizes via per-category keyword sets.

Each skill is a folder skills/<lowercase-hyphen-name>/ containing one SKILL.md with deliberately minimal YAML frontmatter — ONLY two fields: `name` (must match dir exactly) and `description` (a multi-line `>` block that bakes the trigger phrases right in, e.g. JTBD's 'Use when asked about jobs-to-be-done, why customers switch products...'). Auto-invocation is purely description-match; no explicit trigger/when fields. Body follows a fixed 3-part shape (opening statement -> Core Framework section -> Guidelines), 50-150 lines, 'no fluff'. Frameworks are encoded directly: feature-prioritization = RICE (Reach x Impact x Confidence / Effort) + Linear's blocker/enabler tiebreaker; jtbd-analysis = JTBD job statements + Forces of Progress (Push/Pull/Anxiety/Habit); user-interview = Mom Test five-question ladder; prd-writing = evidence-first PRD.

Pattern_raw_name: ALWAYS/NEVER/CRITICAL rule blocks + required output sections
How_implemented: Every skill expresses its method as imperative rules, not prose suggestions — e.g. jtbd CRITICAL: 'NEVER define jobs as features... The job is I want to share a polished document with my investors', NEVER 'interview only happy customers', ALWAYS 'identify the trigger event'. feature-prioritization enforces an output contract: confidence never 100% without quantitative evidence, 'All calculations must be shown explicitly', no blocker may rank below an enabler without justification. prd-writing mandates fixed sections (Problem, Goals, Target Users, Requirements with P0/P1/P2, Success Metrics WITH counter-metrics, Out of Scope, Open Questions). The counter-metric requirement and 'show your math' rules are a light anti-hallucination / gap-discipline gate.

A subset of skills bundle a references/ subdirectory with a reusable template/artifact the agent fills in — user-interview ships interview-guide-template.md alongside its SKILL.md. This is the method-file + template-file pairing, but applied selectively (most skills, e.g. opportunity-mapping, are SKILL.md only); there are no separate worked-example files, so it is a partial Skill+Template pattern rather than a full Skill+Template+Example triad.

Single-plugin marketplace manifest: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json declares one plugin `product-skills` whose `skills` array explicitly enumerates all 16 ./skills/<name> paths with strict:false, installable via `/plugin marketplace add assimovt/productskills`. Also distributed via a CLI package manager (`npx skills add assimovt/productskills`, all-or-pick-specific), git submodule, or direct clone into .claude/skills/. README claims the plain-markdown skills are portable to Cursor/Codex/Devin — cross-harness by virtue of being framework-neutral markdown rather than via per-harness adapters.

Defines a manual quality gate (no CI/automated validator): name-matches-directory, description must include trigger phrases, grounded in a real citable framework, uses ALWAYS/NEVER/CRITICAL not suggestions, examples over explanations, assumes 3-50-person teams, no 'You are an expert' role-setting, no corporate PM language (SAFe/velocity/story points/RACI), no implementation/code, 50-150 line body. Enforced by human PR review against the checklist, not by a linter.

Pattern_raw_name: Three-tier skill taxonomy: component / interactive / workflow
How_implemented: Every skill is a folder skills/<name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, intent [block scalar], type [component|interactive|workflow], theme, best_for[], scenarios[], estimated_time) and a fixed 6-section body (Purpose, Key Concepts incl. anti-patterns, Application, Examples, Common Pitfalls, References). `type` is the orchestration axis: component = single artifact (5-90 min, e.g. user-story), interactive = conversational elicitation (e.g. prioritization-advisor), workflow = multi-day orchestration that references component+interactive skills. ~49-52 skills. The frontmatter `description` is the auto-invocation trigger (description-match).

Pattern_raw_name: Adaptive Decision Ladder (interactive skills, e.g. prioritization-advisor)
How_implemented: Interactive skills' Application section is a scripted elicitation ladder: 'asks up to N adaptive questions, offering 3-4 enumerated options at each step.' Each question is a named decision dimension (Q1 Product Stage, Q2 Team Context, Q3 Decision-Making Needs, Q4 Data Availability), each presenting numbered options with an inline rationale gloss (e.g. '1. Pre-product/market fit — "..." (High uncertainty, need speed)') plus an 'Or describe your ... (custom)' escape hatch and a '[Selection or custom]' response slot. After synthesis the agent returns a recommended framework with rationale tying answers to the choice, implementation steps, an alternative-with-tradeoffs, framework-specific pitfalls, and reassessment triggers. This is a diagnosis/decision skill (matches framework to context) not a doc generator — anti-dogmatic ('no single best framework').

Pattern_raw_name: workshop-facilitation — shared interaction-protocol skill (DRY source of truth)
How_implemented: A reusable skill that other interactive skills cite as 'the default interaction protocol' via a relative link: '[`workshop-facilitation`](../workshop-facilitation/SKILL.md)'. It centralizes the elicitation UX so each advisor skill only carries domain content: session heads-up (estimated duration + question count), three entry modes (Guided step-by-step / Context dump = paste-and-skip-redundant-Qs / Best guess = infer with labeled assumptions), one-question-per-turn rule, progress labels ('Context Q3/8', 'Scoring Qx/5'), multi-select parsing ('1,3' or '1 and 3'), interruption handling (answer meta-Q, restate progress, resume), numbered recommendations ONLY at decision points, and a closing 'Assumptions to Validate' section if best-guess was used. Conflict rule: domain SKILL.md wins. This is meta-DRY: the interaction algorithm lives once, every advisor references it.

Pattern_raw_name: Methodology-encoded component skills (user-story, RICE/ICE/Kano/MoSCoW, JTBD, OST, etc.)
How_implemented: Named PM/biz frameworks are baked into skills as enforced templates with quality contracts. user-story encodes Mike Cohn 'As a / I want / so that' + Gherkin Given/When/Then with explicit validation checks, ✅/❌ paired good-vs-bad examples, warning signs (multiple When/Then → split the story), and a References block linking related skills + external sources (Cohn 2004, INVEST). The skill effectively carries method (Purpose/Key Concepts) + structure (the markdown template block) + worked examples in one file — a skill/template/example triad inside a single SKILL.md.

commands/<name>.md files are user-invoked slash-commands separate from auto-invoked skills. Frontmatter declares name, description, argument-hint, a `uses:` array of the skills it chains, and an `outputs:` list of deliverables. Body = numbered Workflow steps each naming a skill in backticks (e.g. '1. Frame the problem using `problem-framing-canvas`. 2. Plan ... with `discovery-interview-prep`. ...5. Synthesize ... using `discovery-process`.'), checkpoint gates, and 'Next Steps' routing to other commands (/write-prd or /prioritize). This is the commands-vs-skills split plus an explicit skill router declared in YAML.

Pattern_raw_name: Cross-harness packaging + themed plugin packs + Streamlit playground
How_implemented: .claude-plugin/plugin.json (name/description/version/author) makes the library a Claude Code marketplace plugin. Parallel distribution as uploadable zips per platform: pm-skills-starter-pack.zip (Claude Desktop/Web), pm-skills-codex.zip (Codex), and themed packs (Starter/Discovery/Strategy/Delivery/AI-PM/All-Skills 99-all-skills-pack.zip). A local app/ Streamlit interface (main.py + requirements.txt) lets PMs browse/test skills outside any agent — same markdown skills, multiple harnesses.

A build script regenerates catalog/ indexes (yaml + md) from skill/command frontmatter; docs instruct running 'python3 scripts/generate-catalog.py' whenever skills/commands change. Combined with .github/workflows/, this keeps the index and frontmatter in sync — a lightweight skill-catalog contract (index never drifts from the skills).

One SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, type: workflow — note: NO trigger/disable-model-invocation fields, just name+description+type). It is a single-skill multi-domain operator: rather than N separate skills, one file internally routes across 7 domains and 40+ frameworks via the Routing Table (SK-12 dispatcher behavior, but to knowledge SECTIONS not to other skills/agents). Bundled resources sit as sibling folders knowledge/ templates/ examples/ (SK-02).

Pattern_raw_name: Knowledge module + Template Index + examples/ triad
How_implemented: Each deliverable is backed by three layers: the method/decision-logic in knowledge/<domain>.md, a structure scaffold in templates/ (12 templates: prd.md, user-story.md, positioning-statement.md, etc., mapped by the Template Index table), and a worked example in examples/ (8 files like saas-health-diagnostic.md, prd-review.md, pm-sprint-idea-to-prd.md) showing Prompt + Example Output Excerpt + 'Why This Matters'. Modules encode named frameworks (JTBD, RICE, Kano, Moore positioning, OST, Cohn+Gherkin, Working Backwards/PRFAQ, Rule of 40).

An always-on 'Framing gate' inspects every artifact request for solution smuggling, missing success metrics, or 3+-feature scope mixing and challenges ONCE before producing — distinct from coaching. The Interaction Protocol picks a mode: direct output / guided (one Q per turn with progress labels Q1/6) / context-dump / best-guess (label every assumption [assumption]). Minor gaps are flagged inline with [flag: ...] rather than blocking. This is elicitation-gating + anti-hallucination labeling baked into one skill.

Every output MUST end with one of STATUS: DONE / DONE_WITH_CONCERNS (name the weakness) / BLOCKED (state what's missing) / NEEDS_CONTEXT (state what would help), followed by a standard close (decisions made / assumptions to validate / next step). Plus a guard: same approach failing 3x → escalate with STATUS: BLOCKED instead of low-quality output. A machine-checkable output contract on a prose skill.

A second behavioral layer that activates ONLY on explicit request ('coach me', '挑战我的想法'). Adds: push back on weak framing, max-2-round follow-up then best-guess, conversation-anti-pattern naming (compliance loop, analysis paralysis, solution fixation), cross-domain gap surfacing, and a verdict (strong/weak/one action). Each knowledge module carries its OWN '## Interaction Rules (Coaching Mode)' table with Trigger / Action / Stop-When columns (e.g. finance: 'churn discussed without cohort breakdown → challenge → stop when user segments'). Reasoning/diagnosis skill, not doc-generation.

Pattern_raw_name: Cross-harness packaging + manual update/install + release validator
How_implemented: package.json + SKILL.md.tmpl + a 'Maintenance Note' make the same skill installable on Claude Code (clawhub), Codex/Cursor/Windsurf (npx skills update), or git clone — markdown-only, no harness-specific adapters needed (SK-16/SK-19). bin/validate-release is a CI-style contract checker: VERSION==package.json version, all required files/dirs exist, package.json files[] covers shipped assets, README trust sections aligned EN/zh, issue template lists all 7 domains, and a regression guard that SKILL.md forbids auto-executing helper scripts (SK-21).

One folder per skill; SKILL.md opens with minimal YAML frontmatter (only `name` + `description`) followed by a body that the linter requires to contain a process/instructions marker plus `## When to Use` and `## When Not to Use` gates. The folder also bundles an `agents/openai.yaml` companion (see cross-harness item). Folder name must equal frontmatter `name`.

Pattern_raw_name: Product/strategy methodology skills (jtbd-extraction, jtbd-clustering, opportunity-solution-tree, ost-intake, ost-target-selection, crux-diagnosis, assumption-mapping/-prioritization, hypothesis-design, experiment-design, mece-analysis, value-chain-mapping, structured-product-strategy, limit-based-strategy, competitor-analysis, prd-writing, spec-writing)
How_implemented: Named PM/strategy frameworks encoded as step procedures. jtbd-extraction bakes in the four JTBD forces (pushes/pulls/habits/anxieties) with an extract→synthesize process; crux-diagnosis defines the 'crux' concept, a current→future causal graph, a 6-criterion blocker scoring rubric (causal centrality, bottleneck severity, solvability, leverage, time-to-impact, evidence strength), and demands 4-7 options incl. one non-obvious + one constraint-relaxation. Each has a strict Output contract.

Pattern_raw_name: Diagnosis/reasoning skills (crux-diagnosis, root-cause-analysis, systematic-debugging, problem-structuring, signal-identification, decision-reversibility, hidden-agendas, meeting-power-dynamics)
How_implemented: A distinct class of reasoning/diagnosis skills (vs doc-generators) — e.g. crux-diagnosis outputs a chosen strategy + scored crux rather than a template fill; systematic-debugging/root-cause-analysis drive investigation. AGENTS.md routes coding work to systematic-debugging→verification.

Pattern_raw_name: Enforced Output + dual usage gates per skill
How_implemented: Every skill ends with an `## Output` block enumerating required deliverables (crux-diagnosis: exec summary, evidence pack, crux definition+scoring, options table, chosen strategy, 3-5 strategic moves with owners/timelines) and mandatory `When to Use`/`When Not to Use` sections. The linter (validate_skills.py) fails the build if any of these structural sections is missing, making the contract machine-enforced.

ost-intake parses provided context FIRST, then asks concise questions ONLY for the missing of its four required fields and stops as soon as all four are complete — a bounded clarify-before-output gate that also reframes solution-flavored outcomes into measurable ones and feature-like nodes into moments-in-time (normalization = gap-flagging on input quality).

Each skill folder carries an `agents/openai.yaml` sidecar (name, description, version:1, invocation.trigger: implicit|explicit) — an OpenAI/Codex-format manifest paralleling the Claude-style SKILL.md so the same skill runs across runtimes. The linter enforces name==folder, version==1, and trigger ∈ {implicit, explicit}.

Pattern_raw_name: AGENTS.md skill router table + execution-first routing policy
How_implemented: AGENTS.md holds a category→skills→when-to-use table (planning/building/analysis/research/decisions/strategy/meetings/stakeholders) and an explicit 'Skill Routing Policy (Execution-First)' that escalates by task size: small→direct edit/systematic-debugging→verification; medium→writing-plans→tdd→verification; large→PRD/spec first. Skills are invoked by natural reference ('Run root cause analysis', 'Analyze using JTBD').

A real CI guardrail: iterates every .agents/skills/* dir and asserts SKILL.md exists, has valid `---` frontmatter, name matches folder, has a description, contains a process/instructions marker, and has both When-to-Use sections; requires the agents/openai.yaml sidecar with matching name/version/trigger; and greps ALL repo *.md for stale path references (`Skills/`, `sync_skills.sh`, raw `.claude/skills/`). Exits non-zero with an itemized FAIL list.

Loads Evals/skills/cases/*.json, gets a model response either from a live OpenAI-compatible endpoint (temp 0, execution-focused system prompt) or a recorded fixture (Evals/skills/fixtures/<skill>.json), and scores via token-overlap of expected vs response, writing pass/fail + checks to results/. A fixture-or-live design lets the eval run deterministically in CI without API keys.

Separate eval that scores routing accuracy: KEYWORD_RULES maps each skill (verification/tdd/writing-plans/systematic-debugging/brainstorming) to trigger phrases, route_skills() picks skills from a prompt, and cases in Evals/skills/routing_cases.json are graded against expected routes with a configurable --min-pass-rate. Treats skill selection itself as a testable behavior.

Pattern_raw_name: SKILL.md + YAML frontmatter (name, description with embedded trigger)
How_implemented: Every skill is skills/<name>/SKILL.md with two required frontmatter fields: name (must equal the directory name) and description. The description deliberately embeds the framework provenance AND the trigger phrase ('...Based on Teresa Torres' Continuous Discovery Habits. Use when structuring discovery work...') so auto-invocation fires on description-match. Skills use NO $ARGUMENTS placeholder (only commands do).

Pattern_raw_name: Methodology-encoded skills (Torres OST, Cagan, Savoia, Olsen, Maurya, Strategyzer)
How_implemented: Skills bake named frameworks into structured bodies: opportunity-solution-tree encodes Torres' 4-level OST (Outcome→Opportunities→Solutions→Experiments) with the Opportunity Score formula Importance×(1−Satisfaction) and the V/U/V/F assumption-risk taxonomy; prioritization-frameworks encodes 9 named methods with literal formulas (RICE=(Reach×Impact×Confidence)/Effort, ICE=Impact×Confidence×Ease). Provenance is named in-frontmatter so the method, not a generic answer, is invoked.

Pattern_raw_name: Standalone reference/diagnosis skills (prioritization-frameworks as comparison guide)
How_implemented: Some skills function as decision aids invoked without any command — prioritization-frameworks is a comparative 'which method when' guide (Opportunity Score for customer problems, ICE for quick triage, RICE for scaled teams) plus four downloadable templates (PDF/Slides/Sheets). It reasons about method selection rather than generating a single doc.

Skill/command bodies specify required output sections (OST → hierarchical markdown of all four levels with hypothesis+success-metric per experiment; /discover → markdown discovery plan with ideas, assumptions matrix, experiment details, timeline, decision rules). Output shape is dictated by the body, not left to the model.

intended-vs-implemented finds the gap between documented intent and actual code 'with cited evidence on both sides'; security-audit-static instructs the model to map trust boundaries, cross-reference documented intent, 'self-refute every finding', and report ONLY evidence-backed risks — an anti-hallucination gate baked into the skill prompt that suppresses unverified findings.

Hard architectural rule in CLAUDE.md: skills = auto-loaded domain knowledge (nouns), commands = user-invoked workflows (verbs) that chain skills. Commands carry a single $ARGUMENTS placeholder (e.g. proofread takes '<text to check>' as pasted text or uploaded DOCX/PDF/MD) and frontmatter of description+argument-hint; skills carry none. Commands may only reference skills inside the same plugin (enforced by the validator).

Root .claude-plugin/marketplace.json lists 9 plugins each with name/description/source-path/category=product-management. Each plugin has .claude-plugin/plugin.json with name (=dir), version (all synced to 2.0.0), description, author object, keywords array, homepage, license=MIT. Installable via 'claude plugin marketplace add phuryn/pm-skills' (Claude Code) or browse-and-add in Cowork.

Same marketplace.json works for Codex CLI (slash commands unavailable there → users invoke via plain language). For Cursor/Gemini etc., README instructs copying skills/*/SKILL.md into the tool's skills dir (.cursor/skills/, .gemini/skills/). The universal SKILL.md markdown format is the portability layer.

A Python validator enforces the contract: SKILL.md exists per skill dir; YAML frontmatter delimited; required fields (skills: name+description; commands: description; plugins: name+version+description); skill/plugin name must equal directory name; semver x.y.z; keywords is an array; warns if description <30 chars or lacks trigger phrases, if skill body <50 or >3000 words; checks commands only reference same-plugin skills; checks README presence + expected sections (overview/install/skill/command). Run after every change; counts and versions bumped together.

Pattern_raw_name: SKILL.md + 2-field YAML frontmatter (name, description) across all 15 skills
How_implemented: Every skill is a folder skills/<name>/ holding SKILL.md plus references/example.md. Frontmatter is intentionally minimal — only name and description (no triggers/tools/model fields). Auto-invocation relies entirely on the description string, which always follows the pattern '<what it does>. Use when <trigger situation>.' (e.g. problem-clarity: 'Use when assessing new feature ideas, startup concepts, or vague user complaints...'). Directory name matches the name field.

The skills produce judgments/verdicts rather than documents: problem-clarity ends in 'Worth Solving / Not Worth Solving'; value-vs-effort ends in 'Build Now / Later / Do Not Build'; launch-readiness in 'Ready / At Risk / Not Ready'; retention-drop-diagnoser yields likely-causes + fix experiments; prd-critic yields a 'Build Readiness: Ready / Needs Revision' verdict. Each is a reasoning aid that forces a discrete decision label, not a PRD/spec emitter.

Pattern_raw_name: Fixed Markdown-heading output contract (## Output with empty ### subheadings)
How_implemented: Each SKILL.md body has an '## Output' section listing required deliverable subsections as bare Markdown headings the model must fill — e.g. prd-critic mandates Strengths / Gaps or Risks / Clarifying Questions / Rewrite Recommendations / Build Readiness; assumption-mapper mandates Key Assumptions / Highest-Risk Assumption / Validation Method / Decision Before Building. Many also bake in a constrained enum (High/Medium/Low; 1-5 score; Ready/At Risk/Not Ready), so the output structure is the contract.

Pattern_raw_name: Named PM methodologies encoded as skills
How_implemented: Frameworks are baked into the skill body: jtbd-extractor encodes Jobs-to-Be-Done (Functional Job / Emotional Job / Success Criteria / Switching Trigger); assumption-mapper encodes the desirability/feasibility/viability risk lens (IDEO/Marty Cagan style) with rank-the-riskiest-assumption-first; value-vs-effort encodes a RICE-like multi-criteria 1-5 scoring (impact/revenue/strategic alignment/confidence/effort).

Pattern_raw_name: Skill + worked-example pair (one example.md per skill)
How_implemented: Every skill ships exactly one references/example.md that is a fully filled-in instance of the output contract, all grounded in the SAME running fictional product ('AI Meeting Notes' for founders). This gives a consistent few-shot anchor across the catalog. It's a Method+Example pair (no separate template file — the template is the empty heading skeleton inside SKILL.md itself), so closer to a duo than a full triad.

README documents one-line install via 'npx add-skill pratikshadake/claude-product-management-skills' (third-party davila7-style installer) rather than a plugin.json/marketplace manifest. No plugin packaging exists in-repo; distribution is git-clone-shaped through the add-skill tool.

Pattern_raw_name: Three-file skill triad: SKILL.md + references/TEMPLATE.md + references/EXAMPLE.md
How_implemented: Every one of the 68 skills is a folder skills/<name>/ with SKILL.md (the methodology/instructions the agent reads) plus references/TEMPLATE.md (output structure to fill) and references/EXAMPLE.md (worked example anchoring the quality bar). The agent reads template+example to calibrate before generating, so outputs mirror a professional example rather than a generic AI average — 'the skill says what a good PRD is, not how to phrase a prompt.'

SKILL.md frontmatter carries name, a long intent-rich description (used by the router for auto-invocation), license, and a metadata block: classification (foundation/phase/utility/tool), version, updated date, category, frameworks: [triple-diamond, okrs, lean-startup, ...], author. The frameworks/classification fields are what the phase-map and validators key off of.

Pattern_raw_name: Methodology-encoded PM skills (Triple Diamond, JTBD, OKRs, Lean Canvas, Foundation/Design Sprint)
How_implemented: Skills bake named PM/biz frameworks directly into the method: 30 phase skills map to Triple-Diamond phases (Discover/Define/Develop/Deliver/Measure/Iterate), foundation skills encode OKRs/personas/lean-canvas/JTBD, and 15 tool skills are workshop methodologies (Foundation Sprint family of 7, Design Sprint family of 7, Note-and-Vote). foundation-okr-writer itself is a multi-mode operator (Guided/One-Shot/Sustained-Coach/Audit/Rewrite) routing internally.

Skills encode hard refusal/labeling rules as part of the method: foundation-okr-writer refuses to fabricate baselines/targets (marks them assumption/placeholder/recommended-to-measure), refuses OKR-scores-for-compensation, requires guardrail counter-metrics on optimization KRs, and reframes feature-delivery KRs into outcome KRs. deliver-prd enforces 7 mandatory sections and a 15-min-readability quality checklist. These are evidence-bound output contracts, not style suggestions.
