foundation-okr-writer scores each OKR set on an 11-criterion rubric (strategic fit, outcome measurement, baseline/target/evidence clarity, focus caps of 1-3 objectives & 2-4 KRs, guardrails, integrity, empowered-team disclosure) with each criterion rated pass/risk/fail plus remedial guidance — a diagnosis/audit skill mode rather than pure doc generation.

scripts/ holds a large CI validator battery (each with a .test.mjs): check-sample-counts (frontmatter counts vs README), check-trigger-fixtures (trigger accuracy — see context_packet pattern below), check-skill-cross-references (broken links/dead anchors, in bash + ps1), check-sample-no-placeholders, check-sample-no-fabricated-metrics, check-sample-exact-quote-sourcing, check-frontmatter-yaml, check-emdash-scars, check-rendered-links, and check-validator-parity (keeps bash/PowerShell/CI inventories in sync). All gate the build; a finding fails CI.

Pattern_raw_name: Trigger fixtures (per-skill eval datasets) + router evals
How_implemented: Each skill ships skills/<name>/evals/trigger-fixtures.json: ≥16 labeled queries (≥8 trigger / ≥8 no-trigger) split ~60/40 train/validation, including near-miss negatives that almost match a 'collision-partner' skill. check-trigger-fixtures.mjs validates schema + quantities + collision-pair coverage; run-trigger-evals.mjs / run-router-evals.mjs actually measure whether the description-based router fires the right skill. This is empirical trigger-accuracy testing of auto-invocation.

Pattern_raw_name: Worked-example sample library across 3 fictional companies (~95 outputs)
How_implemented: library/ holds 95+ sample outputs grouped by three fictional companies at different maturity stages — Brainshelf (early B2C founder, loose hypotheses), Storevine (mid-market e-commerce SaaS), Workbench (internal-tools PM with ADRs). Samples deliberately vary in prompt maturity to 'reflect real life.' Validators scan them for placeholders/fabricated metrics/quote sourcing so the few-shot grounding stays trustworthy.

commands/ holds explicit slash-command mappings (user-invoked) distinct from auto-invoked skills; /chain runs ad-hoc multi-skill chains routed through the pm-workflow-orchestrator, and workshop-methodology slash commands trigger sprint families. The orchestrator + router form the dispatcher layer over the skill catalog.

Pattern_raw_name: Meta-tooling skills (skill builder, validator, workflow builder, diagram gen)
How_implemented: The 12 utility skills are meta-skills: skill builders that scaffold new skills in the triad format, validators, workflow builders that compose chains, changelog curation, and diagram generators — i.e. skills that produce or govern other skills/workflows.

Pattern_raw_name: Cross-harness packaging (Claude plugin / Codex / skills CLI / AGENTS.md / MCP)
How_implemented: Same skills install four ways: Claude Code plugin via marketplace (/plugin marketplace add product-on-purpose/agent-plugins), cross-agent via npx skills add, git clone, and AGENTS.md/MCP discovery. .claude-plugin/ and .codex-plugin/ hold per-harness manifests; agentskills.io-spec compliant. Native sub-agents on Claude Code degrade to inline dispatch skills at skills/utility-pm-{role}/ on other clients.

Single SKILL.md (no separate template/example files — they're inlined). Frontmatter: name, description='When the user needs to define a product feature...', related: roadmap-planning/mvp-scoping, reads: startup-context. Body = 5-step workflow (1 clarify scope → lightweight 2-3pg vs full 5-8pg trigger; 2 gather inputs; 3 draft 8-section PRD; 4 flag top-3 assumptions WITH supporting evidence + invalidation criteria; 5 review/iterate). Enforced 8-section output contract, two inlined worked examples (Slack notifications=lightweight; self-serve onboarding fixing 60% abandonment=full), and hard principles: 40% on 'why' before 'what', one primary objective, data-driven metrics ('D7 retention 25%→35%' not vague), explicit out-of-scope list, relative timeframes not calendar dates. Step 1 is an interactive elicitation gate (lightweight-vs-full); INSPIRED/RICE-adjacent methodology.

8-step workflow encoding named methodology: convert output→outcome statements ('Enable [segment] to [outcome] so that [impact]'), RICE scoring, dependency mapping (technical/data/design/business), Now(0-6wk)/Next(6-12wk)/Later(12+wk) horizons, capacity validation, explicit 'Not Doing' reasoning. Two worked examples; reads startup-context for stage/team/metrics/OKRs.

6-step diagnostic workflow with a rigid output contract: Market Overview + 5 standardized Competitor Profile tables (Company Profile/Core Strengths/Product Gaps/Business Model & Pricing/Competitive Threats) + Differentiation Opportunities + Positioning Recommendation with 12-18mo risks. Principles enforce primary-source research, 'do nothing' as the main competitor, compete on a different axis, honest gap self-assessment.

50 sibling skills/<name>/SKILL.md folders spanning fundraising/sales/product/recruiting/eng/legal/ops/CS/marketing. CONTRIBUTING.md enforces a uniform contract: frontmatter must have name/trigger/related/reads; body must have 7 fixed sections (When to Use, Context Required, Workflow [numbered], Output Format, Frameworks & Best Practices, Related Skills, Examples); every skill MUST list startup-context in `reads`; hyphenated-lowercase naming; alphabetical README table entry; 'test with real founder tasks before submission'. This is a documented authoring/quality contract (manual, not CI-enforced).

Install via `npx skills add shawnpang/startup-founder-skills`, with `--skill <names>` to cherry-pick and `--list` to enumerate. The `skills` package manager is harness-agnostic (plain SKILL.md, no .claude-plugin manifest), so the same pack installs across agents that read SKILL.md. MIT licensed.

Each skill is a folder with SKILL.md + YAML frontmatter (name/description/user-invocable/allowed-tools). `atlas` is a thin alias that immediately delegates to `go` via the Skill tool ('do not duplicate that routing here'). `go` is a pure router that reads current_phase() and dispatches to the matching phase skill, re-checking after each return. This is a deliberate facade→dispatcher→specialist skill chain where the orchestrator survives context loss because routing state lives in a file, not in the skill.

Interactive mode delegates to superpowers:brainstorming for adaptive Q&A (intercepting before it chains to writing-plans); autonomous mode self-brainstorms a structured template and writes session-context/discovery-{timestamp}.md with explicit assumptions for later audit. Classifies project Scale (Solo 8-12 / Team 12-20 / Enterprise 20-30 tasks) which downstream sets task count. Exits only via advance_phase carrying typed evidence (user_approved, constraints_captured, scale, assumptions_documented) — never calls GENERATE directly.

Fills a comprehensive vs minimal template (chosen by answer richness), then runs validate_prd which scores deterministic checks into EXCELLENT (91%+) / GOOD (83-90) / ACCEPTABLE (75-82) / NEEDS_WORK (<75). HARD gate: placeholders_found == 0 — bare TBD/TODO/{{...}} floor the grade to NEEDS_WORK and exit non-zero, but a deferred decision tagged with an explicit `reason:` passes. Then calc_tasks() sets count, parse-prd writes tasks.json, `rate` produces a complexity report, and `expand` (serial/atomic — parallel per-ID is banned for race safety) guarantees every task has subtasks. Coverage is verified by reading tasks.json directly, never the CLI list output.

A first-match decision tree picks exactly ONE execution mode from installed capabilities: Mode C Verified Loop (superpowers+ralph-loop) → Mode A Plan&Drive (superpowers only) → Mode B Auto-Execute (task-master-ai only) → Mode D Atlas Fleet (premium tier + parallelizable graph; free-tier sees a locked teaser). It mandates AskUserQuestion to present the recommendation + alternatives (even external tools like Cursor/RooCode/Gemini) — the structured choice is the durable handoff record, deliberately replacing 'prose affirmatives' as an anti-pattern.

Pattern_raw_name: execute-task — 13-step CDD-gated build loop ending in SHIP_CHECK_OK
How_implemented: A 13-step loop moves exactly one task pending→done per iteration: heartbeat cron, inbox/TodoWrite drift check (>5 variance halts), next-task selection, plan matching, CDD card generation (subtasks→verifiable checks at .atlas-ai/cdd/task-<id>.json), status flip, complexity-tiered subagent dispatch, then TRIPLE verification — mandatory hard exit-code gate (Gate 5, 'NO override path, unfakable') + reachability sweep (WIRED/EXEMPT/ORPHAN/ERROR) + three independent checkers. Blocked >15min escalates a stepback ladder (/stepback→/research-before-coding→/question→pivot). Terminates by running .atlas-ai/ship-check.py (exit 0), calling Skill(sync) to refresh memory, then printing SHIP_CHECK_OK exactly once.

Calls compute_fleet_waves() to slice tasks.json into file-disjoint frontiers, spawns model-routed workers in isolated worktrees, and merges verified branches sequentially into fleet-integration behind checker gates between merges — never touching main until a final green PR. Six hard pre-launch gates (premium tier, inbox ready, task+complexity reports present, clean git, integration-branch policy) must pass before any worker launches. Completion requires a CDD card; missing cards block merge regardless of worker status text.

Pattern_raw_name: expand-tasks — research enrichment via dispatched subagents
How_implemented: For each task, script.py gen-prompt --task-id builds a research prompt; up to 5 research-expander agents run in parallel WITHIN a dependency wave, waves serial between each other. Each agent's output is saved to /tmp/research-task-<ID>.md then atomically written into the task's research_notes via script.py write-research; sub-5-line results trigger a fresh re-research. Backend research path prefers free/local Perplexity proxy over paid APIs.

A single skill folder whose SKILL.md frontmatter (name, description scoped to 'software delivery lifecycle' with explicit exclusions for debugging/testing/PR-review/raw-GitHub-ops) is an auto-invoked router. The body is an intent->reference table mapping user phrasings ('write a PRD', 'break down epic', 'sync to GitHub', 'start issue N', 'standup') to one of five phase reference docs. So one skill covers the whole multi-phase workflow by internally dispatching to bundled references + scripts rather than being many separate skills.

A single shared reference that every phase loads: defines required frontmatter schemas (PRD: name/description/status/created; epic: +progress/prd-link/github-url; task: +depends_on/parallel/conflicts_with), path conventions, the real-timestamp rule (`date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ`), and GitHub guardrails. Acts as the enforced-output contract that keeps generated files machine-parseable by the bash scripts.

PRD creation has a mandatory elicitation step BEFORE writing — agent must ask 'What problem does this solve? Who are the users? What does success look like? What's out of scope? What constraints (tech/time/resources)?' Quality gates enforce that user stories carry acceptance criteria and success metrics are measurable, blocking placeholder content. Encodes a lightweight PRD/discovery methodology.

Bundled runnable bash (status.sh, standup.sh, blocked.sh, next.sh, epic-list/show/status.sh, prd-list/status.sh, search.sh, validate.sh, init.sh, help.sh) the skill shells out to. validate.sh does consistency checks across files (e.g. circular-dependency detection is flagged as an error in structure.md), giving the framework a self-validation contract.

Every skill is a folder whose SKILL.md opens with minimal frontmatter — name (must match dir, regex bmad-[a-z0-9-]+) and a description that states function + an explicit 'Use when the user asks to talk to John / requests the product manager' trigger (≤1024 chars). Folder bundles the method (SKILL.md), structure (template.md), gating (checklist.md, discover-inputs.md) and config (customize.toml) — e.g. bmad-create-story ships SKILL.md+checklist.md+template.md+discover-inputs.md+customize.toml.

bmm-skills is numbered by SDLC phase; within each, agent-* persona skills act as routers that present a numbered menu (from {agent.menu}) and dispatch to component workflow skills via skill:name syntax. PM's menu: PRD→bmad-prd, CE→bmad-create-epics-and-stories, IR→bmad-check-implementation-readiness, CC→bmad-correct-course. Menu accepts number, code, or fuzzy description match; if intent is already clear it jumps straight to the skill.

PM customize.toml bakes named frameworks into the persona: identity 'Thinks like Marty Cagan and Teresa Torres, writes with Bezos's six-pager discipline'; principles 'PRDs emerge from user interviews not template filling / ship the smallest thing that validates the assumption / user value first, feasibility is a constraint.' PRFAQ skill encodes Amazon Working-Backwards.

Demonstrates a versioned compatibility pattern: bmad-create-prd is a thin shim that resolves legacy customize overrides, emits a deprecation warning, and forwards to bmad-prd with create intent + pre-resolved legacy fields (activation_steps_prepend/append, persistent_facts, on_complete), telling users to migrate their .toml. Shows how skill chaining survives consolidation across v6→v7.

Pattern_raw_name: Enforced output contract: story template + checklist gate
How_implemented: Story template.md mandates sections (header w/ status, user-story w/ {{role}}/{{action}}/{{benefit}}, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks with (AC: #) back-references, Dev Notes, References citing source paths+sections, Dev Agent Record). bmad-create-story validates the filled story against ./checklist.md before flipping status to 'ready-for-dev' — a placeholder-proof completion gate; References section forces source citation (anti-hallucination).

Dedicated core skill that runs structured elicitation (clarifying-question loops) callable mid-workflow by any agent before producing output — the framework's interactive-gate primitive, separated from doc-generation skills.

Diagnosis (not generation) skills: validate-prd checks a PRD against a checklist; check-implementation-readiness verifies PRD+UX+Architecture+Epics alignment before coding starts (IR menu item). Reasoning/gating skills distinct from doc producers.

Deterministic 12-rule validator + optional inference pass, emitting structured JSON. Enforces: name regex + dir match; description has 'Use when' trigger ≤1024 chars; non-empty body; step files named step-NN-description.md with goal sections + ## NEXT links; internal refs relative (./ ../), external refs prefixed {project-root}/config var, inter-skill refs ONLY via skill:name (no direct file paths into other skills); all variables resolve to a defined source. Wired into 'npm run quality' / quality.yaml.

Agent activation step-1 resolves a 3-layer override merge — base skill defaults → team config → user config (_bmad/custom/<skill>.toml) — via a Python resolver (or manual merge). Users override persona role/identity/style, persistent_facts, activation prepend/append steps and on_complete WITHOUT forking the skill; non-configurable identity (name John, icon 📋) is locked. A self/team-tunable skill layer.

Pattern_raw_name: Web bundles (bundles.json) for Gemini Gems / ChatGPT Custom GPTs
How_implemented: web-bundles/ packages planning skills (brainstorming-coach, product-brief-coach, prfaq-coach, prd-coach, ux-coach, market-research) as self-contained installs for web LLMs, indexed by bundles.json — so the same skill runs in a $20 ChatGPT/Gemini subscription for planning, then work transitions into the coding harness (cost/load distribution).

marketplace.json defines installable Claude-Code plugins (bmad-method-lifecycle v6.8.0 listing all phase skills; bmad-pro-skills bundling the 12 core skills). bmad-modules.yaml is the installer registry (module ordering, stable/next channels, deprecation messages, post-install guidance, marketplace-plugin resolution). 'npx bmad-method install' supports non-interactive CI install with --set module flags.
bmad-customize edits agent persona/config; the BMad Builder (BMB) module is a meta-toolkit for creating new custom agents and workflows — skills that generate project-specific skills/agents.

Pattern_raw_name: agent-os slash commands (discover-standards, index-standards, inject-standards, plan-product, shape-spec)
How_implemented: Five markdown command files in commands/agent-os/, installed to .claude/commands/agent-os/ as user-invoked slash commands (explicit /commands, not auto-fired skills). Each encodes a numbered multi-step procedure with confirmation gates: discover-standards runs a 6-step ask→draft→confirm→create cycle PER standard (interactive elicitation: 'ask why, then draft', 1-2 clarifying Qs before writing each standard); shape-spec is a 9-step gated workflow with an enforced output contract (mandatory Task-1-saves-docs, fixed 5-file folder shape). Commands chain: discover → index → inject form the standards lifecycle; plan-product → shape-spec form the spec lifecycle.

/inject-standards detects which of three scenarios the user is in — (1) regular conversation, (2) authoring a Claude Code Skill, (3) planning/shaping a spec — and formats the injected standards differently per scenario (inline reference vs embedded into a .claude/skills/ file vs embedded into a spec). It also surfaces relevant .claude/skills/ as implementation aids. Supports auto-suggest mode (analyze convo, propose 2-5, confirm) and explicit mode (`/inject-standards api/response-format`, `api`, or `root`). Acts as a dispatcher routing standards into the right destination by context.

Single skill with YAML frontmatter (name, description, allowed-tools: Bash/Read/Write/Glob/Grep/WebFetch/WebSearch). One skill internally routes across 8 PRD types and 8 execution steps — a single-skill multi-domain operator. Folder bundles 6 reference docs + skill-config.json. Enforced-output contract: 64 numbered rules + mandatory post-generation self-check before delivery.

Pattern_raw_name: Mandatory infinite clarification loop (Rule 1) + Feasibility Gate (Rule 0)
How_implemented: Before any generation the skill MUST ask clarification questions via the AskUserQuestion tool only (2-4 options, ≤12-char headers, 1-4 Qs/call), looping 'infinite rounds' until the user types proceed/generate/start, then immediately jumps to generation with no recap. Rule 0 adds a scope-negotiation gate that fires on big scope (>50 SP) offering Full-Scope-Overview vs Focused-Epic-PRD before clarification.

Rules grouped: Structural Integrity (1-9, e.g. story-point arithmetic must balance, no self-referencing deps, no placeholder TODO tests), Traceability & Verification (10-17, every FR cites a source, unsourced → [SUGGESTED] subsection, honest verdict taxonomy PASS/SPEC-COMPLETE/NEEDS-RUNTIME/INCONCLUSIVE/FAIL), Code Quality (18-30), Security & Compliance (31-46), Documentation & Testing (47-64). A self-check pass validates all rules and corrects violations before the 9 files are delivered — a placeholder-proof gate with anti-hallucination [SUGGESTED] labelling.

A named strategy library (Plan-and-Solve, Tree-of-Thoughts, ReAct, Reflexion, Few-Shot, Meta-Prompting, Verified Reasoning/CoV, Graph-of-Thoughts, Self-Consistency, Buffer-of-Thoughts, Confidence Fusion, etc.), each tagged to a peer-reviewed benchmark. Algorithm 14 'Research-Weighted Selector' matches claim characteristics → strategy; Algorithm 16 injects strategy guidance into prompts; Algorithm 17 tracks actual vs expected confidence gain and flags underperformers. Exposed via MCP list_available_strategies (tier-gated).

OrchestrationEngine enforces a hard guardrail: any step exceeding 5 minutes outputs partial work and advances; tool failure tries one alternative then proceeds; 200k token budget; never batch-generate silently, never ask 'shall I write the files'. Turns the workflow into a non-blocking state machine.

The Claude Code plugin ships ~54 command .md files (packages/claude-code-plugin/commands/) — one per Task Master operation (parse-prd, expand-task, expand-all-tasks, analyze-complexity, add/remove-dependency, validate-dependencies, fix-dependencies, status-transition commands like to-done/to-review, next-task, etc.). Each is a thin prompt wrapper that maps a slash command to the underlying `task-master <cmd> --arg=$ARGUMENTS` CLI/MCP call. These are explicit user-invoked commands (the SK-20 commands side) rather than auto-fired skills.

expand-task encodes a typed work-breakdown method: it produces 3-7 subtasks of 1-4h each, and selects a decomposition template by work type — Feature: Setup→Implement→Test→Integrate; Bug: Reproduce→Diagnose→Fix→Verify; Refactor: Analyze→Plan→Refactor→Validate. analyze-complexity scores each task 1-10 and emits recommendedSubtasks + an AI-generated expansionPrompt (TaskComplexityData) that pre-seeds the expansion — a reasoning/diagnosis step whose output contract drives the next generation step.

command-pipeline.md defines a mini-DSL for chaining commands with state-passing: arrows (→) sequence steps, `$count=pending-count` stores values, `if:cond→action→else→fallback` and `for:items→process` give control flow, `[analyze|test|lint]` runs branches in parallel and `join:report` consolidates. Example: `parse-prd → expand-all → filter:complex>70 → assign:senior → sprint-plan:weighted`. smart-workflow.md adds heuristic routing: it inspects the last command + time-of-day + task inventory to recommend the next command (e.g. morning→standup→next→start), acting as a dispatcher over the command set.

parse-prd accepts a `research` keyword and `comprehensive` modifier in its argument string; a separate parse-prd-with-research command routes PRD parsing through a research-capable provider (e.g. Perplexity) to ground task generation in external info before structuring tasks.

Workflow prompt bodies in src/core/templates/workflows/{propose,explore,apply-change,archive-change,...}.ts are rendered into per-harness command files (e.g. .claude/commands/opsx/<id>.md) with YAML frontmatter (name/description/category/tags). Each command is a thin orchestration shim: it instructs the agent to clarify intent, call `openspec new change`, query the artifact graph via CLI, and generate artifacts in dependency order using templates as structure. Commands (user-invoked /opsx:*) are cleanly split from the CLI's terminal verbs (openspec init/validate/update).

schemas/spec-driven/templates/{proposal,spec,design,tasks}.md pair a worked structure (sections + HTML-comment guidance placeholders) with the schema.yaml instructions string (the method) for each artifact. Templates enforce an output contract: proposal must have Why/What Changes/Capabilities(New|Modified)/Impact; spec scenarios MUST use exactly 4 hashtags (####) with WHEN/THEN; requirements use '### Requirement:' + SHALL/MUST; tasks must be `- [ ] X.Y` checkboxes. The agent is told to treat templates as structure guides, never copy the rules into output.

Pattern_raw_name: Interactive explore command (thinking-partner gate before propose)
How_implemented: /opsx:explore (src/core/templates/workflows/explore.ts) is a deliberate pre-commit reasoning step — a thinking partner that helps the user shape intent BEFORE a change folder is scaffolded, separating divergent diagnosis from the committed propose->apply pipeline.

`openspec validate <change>` (src/commands/validate.ts + src/core/validation) checks the delta spec formatting (requirement headers, scenario hashtag depth, kebab-case capability names matching the proposal contract), enforcing the structural output contract as a CI/linter-style check rather than trusting the model.

src/core/command-generation/adapters/ holds 27 adapter files (claude, cursor, codex, gemini, github-copilot, windsurf, cline, roocode, kilocode, amazon-q, qwen, etc.), each registered in registry.ts and emitting the same /opsx workflow commands into that tool's native command dir/format (Claude -> .claude/commands/opsx/<id>.md with frontmatter). available-tools.ts auto-detects which tools a repo uses via detectionPaths/skillsDir. One workflow definition, 27 install targets, regenerated by `openspec update`.

Pattern_raw_name: Pluggable workflow schemas (third-party schema bundles + profiles)
How_implemented: The spec-driven schema.yaml is one named, versioned workflow profile; src/core/artifact-graph/resolver loads schemas from project/user/package dirs, and the README describes distributing alternative opinionated workflows as standalone third-party schema bundle repos selected via `openspec config profile`. The workflow itself (artifacts, ordering, instructions) is data, so teams can author their own propose/apply machine without forking the CLI.

Pattern_raw_name: templates/commands/*.md (constitution, specify, clarify, plan, tasks, analyze, checklist, implement)
How_implemented: Each is a Markdown command with YAML frontmatter: `description`, optional `scripts:` (sh + ps variants, e.g. scripts/bash/setup-plan.sh --json), and `handoffs:` listing the next command(s). They use `$ARGUMENTS` placeholder, a `## User Input` block, `## Pre-Execution Checks` (extension hooks), `## Outline`, and `## Mandatory Post-Execution Hooks`. The fixed chain IS the command-chaining workflow; commands are user-invoked slash commands (vs auto-invoked skills).

Pattern_raw_name: handoffs: frontmatter (labeled next-step buttons, send:true auto-advance)
How_implemented: Each command's frontmatter declares typed handoffs to the next agent/command with a label, a pre-filled prompt, and optional `send: true` to auto-advance. E.g. specify.md hands off to speckit.plan ('Build Technical Plan') and speckit.clarify (send:true); tasks.md hands off to analyze (send:true) and implement (send:true). This wires the chain declaratively rather than hard-coding it in prose.

Every command has identical pre/post hook scaffolding: read .specify/extensions.yml, look under hooks.before_<cmd> / after_<cmd>, filter by `enabled`, and for each hook emit either an optional-hook prompt or a mandatory `EXECUTE_COMMAND: {command}` block that the agent MUST actually invoke and await. This lets extensions (Jira, security-review) splice commands into the chain without editing core templates.

Reframes checklists as requirements-quality unit tests (clarity/completeness/consistency/coverage/edge-cases of the REQUIREMENTS), explicitly NOT implementation verification. Generates up to 3 (then up to 5) dynamic clarifying questions from extracted signals before producing items. /implement later scans checklists/*.md, counts [ ]/[X], builds a PASS/FAIL table, and STOPS to ask the user before implementing if any are incomplete — an output/quality gate.

Runs a structured ambiguity scan across a fixed taxonomy (Functional Scope, Domain/Data Model, Interaction/UX, Non-Functional, Integration, Edge Cases, Constraints, Terminology, Completion Signals), marks each Clear/Partial/Missing, then asks up to 5 highly-targeted questions and encodes answers back into spec.md. Designed to run BEFORE /plan, warning that skipping increases downstream rework risk.

Pattern_raw_name: [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] / FR-### / SC-### markers in spec-template.md
How_implemented: spec-template seeds requirements as `FR-001: System MUST ...` and success criteria as `SC-001: [Measurable metric]`, plus explicit `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION: ...]` placeholders that /plan converts into research tasks and /clarify resolves. User stories carry Priority P1/P2/P3 and 'mandatory' section markers enforce an output contract.

`uv tool install specify-cli`; `specify init <project> --integration <agent>` scaffolds .specify/ + agent-specific command files; `specify self check|upgrade`; `specify extension/preset/bundle search|add|install`. Extensions add new commands, presets override existing templates, bundles (bundle.yml, version-pinned) provision role-based curated sets, resolved from a priority-ordered catalog stack (project > user > built-in).

Pattern_raw_name: template resolution stack (overrides > presets > extensions > core)
How_implemented: Templates resolve at runtime in priority order: .specify/templates/overrides/ → .specify/presets/templates/ → .specify/extensions/templates/ → .specify/templates/. First match wins; removing a layer activates the next. Commands invoke it as `specify preset resolve spec-template` rather than hard-coding a path — so orgs can swap in compliance-formatted templates without forking.

Pattern_raw_name: cross-harness integration packaging (SkillsIntegration / MarkdownIntegration / TomlIntegration base classes)
How_implemented: One template set is rendered per agent by base classes in src/specify_cli/integrations/: ClaudeIntegration(SkillsIntegration) installs to .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with args '$ARGUMENTS'; TomlIntegration handles Gemini/Tabnine ({{args}}); MarkdownIntegration is the common case. Each declares key/config/registrar_config/context_file and registers in a global registry (_register_builtins). claude/__init__.py also injects a per-command `argument-hint:` into frontmatter and exposes a FORK_CONTEXT_COMMANDS map (currently empty — see new_patterns).

An Agent (tools: Write/Edit/Read/Bash, model sonnet) that, given the finalized context_packet+requirements+architecture, writes 7 project-specific SKILL.md files into .claude/skills/ of the TARGET project. Each is built from an inline template with {var} tokens; a Quality Standard forbids leftover curly-braces ('zero curly-brace syntax in output') and demands real RF-/DR-/ADR- IDs and stack versions — generic templates are banned. mode:update touches only affected skills per a change->skills map and appends Evolution Log rows. This is skills-that-generate-skills.

Generated skill with a Mandatory Pre-Implementation Checklist and a Hard Blocks table (descope without /prd-evolve = blocked; off-stack tech = blocked; separate-repo service = blocked). Carries a condensed RF/DR feature map and a stale-skills check (frontmatter prd_version vs PRD header). Description auto-triggers 'when implementing any feature... in {product_name}'.

The one skill shipped IN the plugin (skills/prd-guardian/SKILL.md). Auto-triggers in any repo containing docs/prd/PRD.md + .claude/skills/project-guardian. Acts as a router/dispatcher: a Graphviz decision graph routes feature/arch/review actions to a fixed Required Check Order over the 5 project skills, does a version-mismatch detection, and defines Hard Stops (no PRD found -> suggest /prd-new).

Generated per-project: project-domain-rules encodes DDD ubiquitous-language tables + invariants + state machines (synonyms-to-avoid column); project-architecture is the canonical stack table + layer dependency rule + 'what requires an ADR'; project-compliance is a per-regulation checklist (GDPR/LGPD/PCI) with Prohibited Patterns (e.g. 'logging PII in plain text'). Named methodologies (Clean Code/Clean Arch/DDD/Pragmatic Programmer/SOLID/12-Factor) are baked into the per-layer CLAUDE.md guides.

project-docs-stack enforces the docs/stack/ pre-search protocol with a Hard Rule against duplicate web searches. project-cicd carries vcs/pipeline_file in frontmatter and a 'when to evolve the pipeline' trigger table + pre-merge consistency checklist (every service has a path-filtered job, no hardcoded secrets).

prd-new conducts a conversational, ONE-question-at-a-time interview across 7 fixed blocks (Identity/Business/Features/Regulatory/Integrations/Infrastructure/Stack), explicitly NOT using AskUserQuestion for blocks (reserved for the single architecture-approval gate in Phase 4). Numbered-option prompts with '(Or type your own answer)'. Early-termination handling: 'skip' marks remaining blocks coverage_score:0 and records Known Gaps in PRD §2.4. After each block, pending_research is drained by dispatching researchers.

SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name + description). Encodes the cross-cutting Message Bus protocol (slug resolution, read-before-write, clear-before-compose) that the initiate/check/handoff commands cite by reference rather than re-spelling — a shared procedure doc consumed by every agent. Installed into the project (not optional).

Frontmatter name/description; body is a 6-section operator manual that teaches an agent to work inside a FORKED APM repo: map of `templates/*` source dirs, build-time placeholder resolution ({VERSION}, {SKILL_PATH:name}) via build-config.json, the spec-first change rule, and the build→test→tag→release cycle. Effectively a meta-skill for generating/customizing the framework's own templates and producing installable releases.
General-purpose APM helper: explains concepts, detects installed APM version, supports v0.5.x migration, answers documentation Q&A. Installed on-demand into platform-specific skill dirs.

Nine ordered slash commands as markdown-with-frontmatter form the user-invoked control surface: 1-initiate-planner, 2-initiate-manager, 3-initiate-worker, 4-check-tasks, 5-check-reports, 6/7-handoff-manager/worker, 8-summarize-session, 9-recover. Each command boots an agent persona, wires it to `.apm/` files and the bus, and chains into the next — an explicit commands(user)-vs-skills(shared procedure) split.

Pattern_raw_name: Procedure guides (`templates/guides/*.md`): context-gathering, work-breakdown, task-assignment, task-execution, task-logging, task-review
How_implemented: Each phase has a dedicated guide the agent reads at the relevant step (the command files stay thin and cite the guide). task-assignment.md encodes the methodology for building SELF-CONTAINED Task Prompts (dependency classification same-agent vs cross-agent, content extraction, enforced YAML frontmatter contract with stage/task numbers + pre-built log_path + dependency flag, and required body sections objective/instructions/workspace/expected-output/validation). Method is separated from the templates it fills.

Same SKILL.md is installed into per-platform dirs (`.claude/skills/`, `.cursor/skills/`, `.github/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`, `.opencode/skills/`) so one skill body runs on Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex CLI, OpenCode, Antigravity. The `apm` npm CLI (`apm init`) is the package-manager installer that lays down commands/agents/skills/.apm scaffolding.

A pure-markdown prompt template ('# Rule: Generating a PRD'). Interactive elicitation gate: the AI *must* ask 3–5 clarifying questions BEFORE writing, focused on the 'what/why' not 'how', with a strict formatting contract — number questions (1,2,3), give lettered options (A,B,C,D), so the user replies '1A, 2C, 3B' (worked Example Format block included). Enforced output contract: a fixed 9-section PRD structure targeted at a 'junior developer' reader. Anti-overreach gate via 'Final Instructions': (1) do NOT start implementing, (2) must ask clarifying questions, (3) revise PRD from answers. Anti-hallucination flavor: only ask when not inferable; surface unknowns in an 'Open Questions' section rather than inventing.

Pure-markdown template that converts a PRD into an executable plan via an explicit two-phase interaction model: Phase 1 generate ~5 high-level parent tasks (always task 0.0 = 'Create feature branch') and STOP with the literal line 'Ready to generate the sub-tasks? Respond with "Go" to proceed.'; Phase 2 only after the user types 'Go', expand each parent into 1.1/1.2 sub-tasks. Enforced output contract: a verbatim markdown skeleton (Relevant Files + Notes about jest test placement + hierarchical checkbox Tasks 0.0/1.0/1.1). It also encodes git hygiene (feature branch first) and TDD-leaning structure (every code file paired with a .test file).

Pattern_raw_name: One-sub-task-at-a-time execution protocol (README + generate-tasks 'Instructions for Completing Tasks')
How_implemented: The execution stage is itself a prompt rule rather than a separate file on current main (the former process-task-list.md content is folded in): the user says 'Please start on task 1.1', the AI does ONE sub-task, marks it `[x]` in the file, then halts for human review/approval before touching the next sub-task. The human checkpoint between every atomic step is the core safety mechanism — it caps blast radius and keeps the human as the loop's clock.

Flat single-file SKILL.md (no frontmatter YAML — uses a plain `## Trigger` line listing natural-language phrases: 'write a PRD', 'spec out [feature]', 'one pager for [feature]'). Mechanism is a 5-step state machine: Step 1 hard elicitation gate ('ask 3-5 clarifying questions... Do NOT proceed until the user answers'); Step 2 stage classification (6-stage table, default Stage 4 Solution Review); Step 3 fixed 8-section output contract (Hypothesis/Problem/Strategic Fit/Solution/Non-Goals/Success Metrics/Rollout/Open Questions) with a 'We believe [action] will [outcome] for [users], measured by [metric]' hypothesis template; Step 4 stage-appropriate verification checklists; Step 5 hands off to reviewer personas. Anti-hallucination via `[NEED: data from X]`. Extensive Good-vs-Bad few-shot pairs for every section + an 'Anti-Patterns (From Analyzing 500+ PRDs)' list. The AI-feature clause demands '15-25 example input/output pairs' as a spec.

Trigger-phrase activation; 3-question context gate; encodes a named 'What They Built / What's Smart (3 nailed) / What's Weak (3 gaps) / Implications for Us (copy/avoid/differentiate)' framework. Enforces specificity with a Bad-vs-Good example pair and Rules demanding numbers (pricing, conversion, market share, review counts) and `[NEED: more info on X]` for gaps; explicitly bans visual-design opinions in favor of product-decision analysis.

4-question context gate (what/date/users-affected/risk). Output is a phase-banded checklist (Pre-Launch / Launch Day / Post-Launch 48h / Post-Launch 1wk) that is RISK-SCALED via a Low/Medium/High table — High adds staged rollout 1%->10%->50%->100%, war-room channel, exec notification, 24h monitoring. Rules enforce verifiability ('every item must be verifiable... Done means someone can confirm it') and named constraints ('Never launch on Friday afternoons', 'if rollback >5 min, add more testing time'). Good-vs-Bad item pairs convert vague items into instrumented, owner-and-threshold-bearing ones.

3-question gate; defines a strict metric taxonomy — Primary / Secondary / Guardrail / Leading Indicators / Anti-Metrics — each requiring exact definition + measurement method + source. Distinctive rule: 'Anti-metrics are mandatory. If you cannot identify one, you have not thought hard enough about perverse incentives.' Flags new instrumentation needs with `[NEEDS INSTRUMENTATION]`. A reasoning/diagnosis skill (forces perverse-incentive thinking) more than a doc generator.

4-input gate (paste backlog/team size/duration/constraints). Produces Sprint Goal + recommended backlog table + an explicit CAPACITY-CHECK arithmetic block (engineers x days x 80% = available, vs planned, with a 20% buffer and Over/Under/Right-sized verdict) + stretch goals + per-sprint risks with mitigations. Hard rule: '80% capacity is the ceiling. Sprints loaded above 80% have a >50% chance of missing the goal.' The capacity math makes it a quasi-executable skill (deterministic calc baked into the prompt rather than code).

3-input gate (paste notes/research question/n participants). Output: Key Findings RANKED BY EVIDENCE STRENGTH, each carrying Finding + Evidence (count + verbatim quote) + Confidence (High/Med/Low) + Product Implication; plus Themes table, Surprises (assumption contradictions), Gaps (uncovered segments/questions), prioritized Actions. Epistemic rules: 'Use actual quotes, never paraphrases'; 'findings from 1-2 participants are signals, not conclusions'; 'distinguish said vs did — behavior outranks stated preference'; every finding needs a confidence level AND a 'so what'. A provenance/confidence-disciplined synthesis skill.

4 blank fill-in markdown structures at REPO ROOT (not bundled inside skill folders). prd-template.md mirrors the prd-writer 8-section schema as an empty scaffold ([Feature Name], 'We believe [action] will [outcome]...'). This realizes a loose Skill+Template+Example triad — but distributed: SKILL.md holds method + worked example, templates/ holds the empty structure, and they are linked only by @-reference convention (README: 'Read @templates/prd-template.md and use that structure'), not by per-skill folder bundling.

Skill = folder .cursor/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name + description; description doubles as the auto-invocation trigger and as the /slash-command). Encodes Teresa Torres' Continuous Discovery Habits method: enforced output contract of 6 named sections (Quick Facts, Memorable Quote ≤125 chars, Story Summary, Experience Map, Opportunities, Insights), a say-vs-do anti-hallucination rule (behavioral evidence over self-report, opportunities require direct quote support with verified attribution), depth variants (Full/Quick/Synthesis-ready), and fixed artifact path user-interviews/snapshots/snapshot-[participant]-[date].md with markdown+YAML frontmatter.

Interactive elicitation gate: BEFORE drafting, asks 3–5 clarifying questions focused on what/why, presented as numbered questions with lettered options so the user can answer compactly ('1A, 2C, 3B'). Enforced 9-section PRD contract (Intro, Goals, User Stories, numbered Functional Requirements, Non-Goals, Design/Tech Considerations, quantified Success Metrics, Open Questions) with a 'junior-developer-ready, unambiguous' quality bar (INSPIRED/spec-driven lineage from ai-dev-tasks). Writes prd-[feature].md to prd/ and chains to generate-tasks.

Methodology-encoded scoring done entirely by prompt instructions (NO bundled .py — purely declarative, unlike repos that ship rice_prioritizer.py). ICE = Impact × Confidence × Ease with explicit mapping tables: Impact maps expected %-metric-change to 0–10, Ease maps person-weeks to 1–10, Confidence aggregates evidence by source type with per-type weights (self-belief 0.01× up to market/user data 3.0×) and group/type caps. Hard anti-hallucination rule: 'Score only on explicitly stated evidence — never invent, infer, or over-credit weak signals.' Outputs ice-[idea]-v[n].md to solutions/ with priority buckets (≥250 immediate, 150–249 test, 100–149 mitigate, <100 hold).
Scaffolding skill (see CM-24) — copies _templates/initiative-template/ to create the per-initiative folder + README + .gitkeep tree. Frontmatter is the standard name/description pair.

20 skills grouped by lifecycle: Discovery (product-diagnostic, create-interview-snapshots, synthesize-snapshots, create-opportunities, generate-solutions, identify-test-assumptions, analyze-metrics), Documents (create-prd, create-one-pager, create-design-brief, generate-figma-prompt, build-prototype), Strategy (product-strategy-review, product-vision-review, okr-sparring-partner, calculate-ice-score), Execution (generate-tasks, one-on-one-meeting, writing-guide, setup-initiative). Each is a /slash-command (user-invocable) AND auto-discoverable by description match. Named PM frameworks baked in: Continuous Discovery (Torres) Opportunity Solution Tree across snapshots→opportunities→solutions→assumptions, ICE, PRISM (in strategy-review).

The product-manager.md agent embeds five fully-specified copy-paste output templates as fenced markdown blocks inside a `## 🛠️ Technical Deliverables` section: a PRD (8 numbered sections incl. evidence table, success-metric table with baseline/target/window, non-goals, RICE), an Opportunity Assessment (with a RICE scoring table R×I×C÷E), a Now/Next/Later Roadmap (incl. a mandatory 'What We're Not Building and Why' table), a GTM Brief, and a Sprint Health Snapshot. These act as enforced output contracts—the method (workflow phases) and the structure (templates) ship together in one file. Frameworks baked in: PRFAQ ('write the press release before the PRD'), RICE, pre-mortem, North Star metric.

Each agent encodes a named multi-phase workflow as a `## 📋 Workflow Process` section. The PM's is a 6-phase lifecycle with concrete gates: Phase 1 mandates ≥5 (ideally 10+) problem interviews before any solution talk; Phase 3 mandates a PRFAQ exercise and an 8-weeks-out pre-mortem; Phase 4 declares a blocker sitting >24h a 'PM failure'. The Critical Rules section adds hard gates ('Lead with the problem not the solution', 'No roadmap item without owner+metric+horizon', 'Validate before you build, measure after you ship').

product-sprint-prioritizer.md bakes a named-framework toolbox into a single agent: RICE, Value/Effort matrix, Kano model, MoSCoW, weighted multi-criteria scoring, plus Scrum/Kanban/SAFe/Shape Up/Design Sprints and OKR tracking—organized under `Prioritization Frameworks` and `Decision Framework` headers. It is a decision/reasoning skill (rank the backlog) rather than a doc-generator.

scripts/convert.sh reads each canonical agent (md + frontmatter: name/description/color/emoji/vibe/tools/body) and emits tool-specific artifacts into integrations/<tool>/: Antigravity/Osaurus → SKILL.md with added risk/source/date_added fields; Cursor → .mdc with globs/alwaysApply; Codex → .toml with toml-escaped developer_instructions; Kimi → split agent.yaml + system.md; OpenClaw → multi-file SOUL.md/AGENTS.md/IDENTITY.md split BY HEADER KEYWORDS (## Identity → SOUL, ## Learning&Memory → AGENTS); Aider/Windsurf → one aggregated CONVENTIONS.md/.windsurfrules. Has slugify(), per-tool prefixing ('agency-'), OpenCode named-color→hex resolution, and a --parallel/--jobs mode. One source of truth, 12 harnesses.

Pattern_raw_name: install.sh interactive multi-tool installer + desktop app
How_implemented: scripts/install.sh + a native desktop app (mac/linux/win) detect installed coding tools, let the user pick divisions/agents selectively, and copy the converted formats into each tool's agent directory in parallel. The repo is effectively a marketplace of agent personas with an install layer over 12+ runtimes.

SKILL.md carries a trigger-rich description ('Trigger on: prioritize my features, RICE scoring, what should I build first...') for auto-invocation; body teaches 7 named, attributed frameworks (RICE=Intercom, ICE=Sean Ellis, Kano, MoSCoW, Opportunity=Ulwick) with formulas + worked examples + a context-based selection decision-tree; each framework points to an `assets/<name>-template.md` fill-in file and a `references/framework-selection-guide.md` deeper guide. Triad of SKILL.md (method) + assets/ (structure templates) + references/ (deep guides) is consistent across all 17 skills.

Pattern_raw_name: SKILL.md + assets/ + references/ triad with on-demand reference loading
How_implemented: Every skill folder is SKILL.md (always-loaded method) + assets/*.md (templates the agent fills) + references/*.md (comprehensive guides). The SKILL.md names each resource inline ('Template: assets/rice-scoring-template.md', 'When you need comprehensive guidance: references/framework-selection-guide.md') so the agent drills into the heavier file only when relevant — progressive disclosure with no vector store.

requirements-engineer + the prd-templates skill encode a deterministic template router: detect 'new product' keywords -> Amazon PR/FAQ; else score Technical/Risk/Scope 0-10, compute avg, apply thresholds (avg<4 & <1wk -> Lean; risk>=8 or scale -> Google; avg<7 -> Comprehensive [default]; else Google) and surface the scored reasoning transparently. PRDs auto-save to prds/<kebab-feature>.md.

A pure reasoning/protocol skill (no templates) auto-loaded by all 6 team agents. Encodes a 3-phase debate (independent investigation -> one-round cross-examination -> weighted synthesis), 5 named cross-examination challenge formats (Evidence/Assumption/Magnitude/Alternative/So-What), explicit debate rules (steel-man before attacking, evidence>assertion, disagree-and-commit, no false consensus), weighted synthesis percentages, dissent-documentation templates, and 5 named anti-patterns (Consensus Theater, Authority Bias, Evidence Arms Race, False Precision). references/team-debate-patterns.md adds Dialectical Inquiry / Devil's Advocacy / Constructive Controversy frameworks.

Backs context-scanner; references/ hold manifest-detection / platform-detection / integration-mapping rules; assets/output-schema.md + presentation-template.md fix the output shape; doctrine 'Facts Not Interpretation / WHAT not HOW / Evidence-Based with Confidence Scores, better to under-report than hallucinate' is an anti-hallucination contract.

Holds the scored output contracts (validation-verdict, prd-review-report, competitive-synthesis templates in assets/) that the lead agent fills during synthesis — separating the deliverable format from the debate logic in team-coordination.

Each skill is a folder under skills/ containing a SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, author, version, tags, repository, license — confirmed on claude-memory-kit). Mostly engineering-domain (python-best-practices, react-patterns, kubernetes-operations, llm-integration, mcp-development). They are vendored copies of external community skills (frontmatter retains the original author/repo), aggregated rather than authored here. No product/PM skill in the set — the PM capability lives entirely in agents/.

README points beyond the 35 bundled skills to '400,000+ additional skills via the SkillKit marketplace' (agenstskills.com), installed with a CLI one-liner: npx skills add <owner>/<repo> (example: npx skills add Sagargupta16/claude-cost-optimizer). So the repo is a thin curated layer over an external package-manager-style skill registry.

Pattern_raw_name: commands/ — 42 slash commands in 8 category folders
How_implemented: Commands live in commands/<category>/ folders (architecture, devops, documentation, git, refactoring, security, testing, workflow) as markdown — the user-invoked /command half of the commands-vs-auto-skills split. The workflow/ folder is the orchestration bucket. No product-management command folder.

SKILL.md whose description is a precise 'use when' trigger list (interpreting a low dimension score, calibrating thresholds, explaining badges to partners). Body encodes the full quality rubric: 10 weighted dimensions, the 3 layers, anti-pattern list (over-constrained directives, empty descriptions, missing 'use when', bloated monolith, orphaned/dead refs), and improvement strategy ('triggering accuracy gives fastest composite gains'). It is the human-readable companion to the executable evaluator — a methodology skill that doubles as a quality contract.

Every skill is plugins/<name>/skills/<n>/SKILL.md with frontmatter name+description and bundled material in references/ subdirs. Description carries the auto-invocation trigger ('Use this skill when...'). 158 such skills across 88 plugins, all generated from this one source shape.

Pattern_raw_name: agent-teams skills (team composition heuristics, task coordination, parallel-debug, multi-reviewer allocation, comms protocol)
How_implemented: Skills folder holds reusable orchestration patterns (team-sizing heuristics, preset compositions, agent-type selection logic) that the /team-* commands and team-lead agent consult — method knowledge separated from the command/agent that invokes it.

Canonical skill format per the example-plugin: a skills/<name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter. Two flavors keyed by frontmatter: model-invoked skills carry name+description (+version) where description is the sole auto-trigger mechanism; user-invoked skills (slash commands) add argument-hint and allowed-tools (a least-privilege tool allowlist). Skills bundle resources in the folder (references/, scripts/). The legacy commands/*.md layout is explicitly documented as loaded identically to a SKILL.md — same engine, different file location — and deprecated in favor of skills/.

A skill whose job is producing other skills, encoding the full loop: capture intent (extract steps from current conversation if user says 'turn this into a skill'), interview for edge cases/IO/success criteria, write the SKILL.md, generate quantitative evals, run claude-with-the-skill over test prompts in the background, and view results via a bundled eval-viewer/generate_review.py script. Ships a separate description-improver script that rewrites a skill's description to be deliberately 'pushy' (to fix Claude's tendency to under-trigger skills). Adapts its jargon to user sophistication. This is the canonical reference for skill-as-evaluated-artifact.

A single command file encodes a fixed 7-phase pipeline (Discovery -> Codebase Exploration -> Clarifying Questions -> Architecture Design -> ...). Each phase has Goal + Actions; Phase 3 (Clarifying Questions) is marked CRITICAL/DO-NOT-SKIP as an interactive elicitation gate before any design; phases 2 and 4 delegate to parallel subagents. Frontmatter is minimal (description + argument-hint, with $ARGUMENTS injected). The command itself is the orchestration spec.

For source repos that ship raw SKILL.md folders with no plugin.json, the marketplace entry sets strict:false and lists an explicit skills:["./skill-a","./skill-b"] array of dirs relative to source.path (paths can reach into multiple library subdirs to expose a curated subset). Each registers as <plugin-name>:<skill-name>. Lets the directory curate arbitrary skill subsets out of any third-party SDK repo without that repo adopting the plugin manifest format.

SKILL.md frontmatter = name + description (trigger-rich: 'Use when turning a vague idea... scoping a feature with goals and non-goals... breaking a big ask into a phased spec') + argument-hint '<feature or problem statement>'. Body encodes a fixed 5-step workflow (understand request -> gather context -> pull from ~~tracker/~~kb/~~design -> generate PRD -> iterate) and an enforced PRD output contract: Problem Statement, Goals (3-5 measurable), Non-Goals (with rationale), persona User Stories in 'As a/I want/so that' + INVEST test, Requirements bucketed P0/P1/P2, leading+lagging Success Metrics, owner-tagged Open Questions, Timeline. Hard rule 'If everything is P0, nothing is P0' enforces ruthless prioritization.

Encodes named research methodology: 5-step Thematic Analysis (familiarize->code->cluster->validate->refine), Affinity Mapping ('one observation per note, do not combine'), Triangulation (method/source/temporal). Personas are behavior-not-demographics, kept to 3-5. Opportunity scoring = addressable users x frequency x severity weighted by evidence strength. Explicit anti-hallucination block: distinguish stated vs actual behavior, surface contradictions as segment signals, attribute quotes to participant TYPE not name, report confidence levels, use ranges not false precision, don't force a narrative. References CONNECTORS.md for placeholder resolution.

Branches by operation type (add/update-status/reprioritize/retimeline/build-from-scratch). Bakes in Now/Next/Later, Quarterly Themes, OKR-aligned, Timeline/Gantt formats and four prioritization frameworks (RICE = (Reach x Impact x Confidence)/Effort, MoSCoW, ICE, Value-vs-Effort 2x2). Governing principle 'roadmaps are zero-sum against capacity — when adding, ask what deprioritizes', plus surface-dependencies-explicitly and resist off-cadence churn.

Two-axis router: update TYPE (weekly/monthly/launch/escalation) x AUDIENCE (exec/eng/cross-functional/customer/board), each with its own template and depth budget (exec <300 words, outcome-focused; eng = technical substance with links/owners/blockers; customer = benefits, no internal jargon; board = metrics+strategy). Status RAG colors (Green/Yellow/Red) + ROAM risk framework (Resolved/Owned/Accepted/Mitigated). Principle: 'lead with the conclusion, not the journey'. Optionally posts via ~~chat.

4-stage flow (scope-to-decision -> research via web search + ~~kb/sales/win-loss -> generate -> follow-up battle-card/exec-summary). Frameworks: weighted Feature Matrix (Strong/Adequate/Weak/Absent), Positioning Analysis (target/category/differentiator/value, find unclaimed positions), Win/Loss methodology, Landscape Mapping on chosen axes. Evidence standard prioritizes real product use + G2/Capterra/TrustRadius reviews + behavioral signals (hiring/funding) OVER marketing claims; instructed to be honest about competitor strengths.

Organizes pulled analytics into a 3-tier hierarchy: North Star -> L1 health (acquisition/activation/engagement/retention/monetization/satisfaction) -> L2 diagnostics (feature usage, segment, funnel). Outputs scorecard + trend + bright-spots + concerns + recommended actions. Hard display rule: 'always show current value, comparison (prev/target/benchmark), trend direction'; mandates segment analysis so aggregates don't mask differences; bans vanity/output metrics; cautions on alert fatigue. Pulls from ~~analytics (Amplitude/Pendo) or asks for manual input.

Gathers team comp / duration / backlog / carryover / blockers, then standalone-mode vs connected-mode (connected auto-pulls backlog from ~~tracker, PTO from ~~calendar, comms via ~~chat). Outputs capacity table, prioritized backlog, risk register, key dates. Principles: plan to 70-80% capacity for interrupts, one focused sprint goal, mark stretch items, honestly diagnose why prior commitments slipped before re-committing. Not advertised in the README's 7-command list — an 8th skill present only in code.

A reasoning/thinking-partner skill (not a doc generator): 4 modes (problem-exploration, solution-ideation = generate 5-7+ distinct approaches via constraint-removal/inversion, assumption-testing, strategy-exploration). Frameworks: How-Might-We, JTBD, Opportunity Solution Trees, First-Principles, SCAMPER, OODA, Reverse Brainstorming. Session arc frame->diverge->provoke->converge->capture. Instructed to be opinionated/constructively challenging ('Assume X — are we confident?'), resist premature optimization and framework-dumping. This is the one skill fronted by an explicit /brainstorm command.

Pattern_raw_name: Canonical SKILL.md = YAML frontmatter (name + description) + markdown body
How_implemented: Every skill is skill-name/SKILL.md opening with a --- fenced YAML block. Only two REQUIRED fields: name (lowercase-hyphen, must equal the folder name) and description (the sole auto-invocation trigger — must state BOTH what it does AND when to use it; 'all when-to-use info goes here, not in the body'). Optional fields seen in the wild: license (docx/brand-guidelines), compatibility. docx's description is a ~120-word keyword-dense paragraph with explicit Do-NOT-use exclusions ('Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets...') — the recommended anti-over/under-trigger style.

Pattern_raw_name: Skill = self-contained folder with bundled scripts/ references/ assets/
How_implemented: Anatomy fixed by skill-creator: scripts/ = executable code for deterministic/repetitive work (run, not read), references/ = docs loaded as-needed, assets/ = files used in output (templates, fonts, icons). docx bundles a full python toolchain (scripts/office/{unpack,pack,validate,soffice}.py + validators/ + helpers/) and tells Claude to call them rather than reinvent; canvas-design bundles ~40 .ttf font files as assets; algorithmic-art ships templates/{generator_template.js,viewer.html}.

template/SKILL.md is a deliberately minimal seed: just frontmatter (name: template-skill, description: 'Replace with...') + '# Insert instructions below'. README documents the two-field minimum and a fuller Examples/Guidelines scaffold. The lowest-friction on-ramp the ecosystem standardizes on.

Pattern_raw_name: skill-creator (meta-skill that authors, evals, benchmarks & packages skills)
How_implemented: A skill whose job is producing other skills end-to-end: capture-intent interview -> draft SKILL.md -> auto-generate 2-3 realistic test prompts into evals/evals.json -> spawn with-skill + without-skill subagents per eval -> grader subagent scores assertions -> aggregate_benchmark.py emits pass_rate/time/tokens mean±stddev+delta -> generate_review.py opens an HTML reviewer -> rewrite -> repeat. Also bundles quick_validate.py (contract check) and package_skill.py (-> installable .skill). Has a dedicated DESCRIPTION-OPTIMIZATION loop: run_loop.py generates 20 should/should-not-trigger queries, 60/40 train/test split, runs each query x3 for trigger-rate, proposes new descriptions for ≤5 iterations, selects best_description by HELD-OUT score to avoid overfitting.

skill-creator's authoring guide prescribes the 'ALWAYS use this exact template' report-structure pattern and an Input/Output few-shot example pattern, while cautioning against heavy-handed ALL-CAPS MUSTs ('explain the why instead'). docx encodes hard contracts inline (e.g. 'CRITICAL: docx-js defaults to A4, not US Letter — always set page size explicitly') plus a post-generation validate.py gate.

Pattern_raw_name: Multi-harness adaptation blocks in one skill
How_implemented: skill-creator carries explicit per-runtime sections — 'Claude.ai-specific instructions' (no subagents -> run tests inline serially, skip benchmark, skip browser viewer) and 'Cowork-Specific Instructions' (subagents yes, but no display -> generate_review.py --static writes standalone HTML; feedback comes back as a downloaded feedback.json). One skill body branches on harness capabilities instead of shipping separate adapters.
